2148:     def remove_tensorify_specialized_graphargs(self) -> None:
2149:         # This is a pretty interesting function. Basically we have this problem
2150:         # where our compiler tends to choke when we have unused inputs. The way
2151:         # we support dynamic float arguments is by doing a joint fx pass and
2152:         # tensorifying away as many symfloats as we can. For the remaining symfloats
2153:         # we have no choice but to specialize... HOWEVER at that point in time
2154:         # we can no longer remove graph inputs. So our sledgehammer solution is to
2155:         # save the state of what inputs we should have specialized in dynamo and
2156:         # restart analysis. This function incorporates this "view from the future"
2157:         # state and specializes inputs that we know we won't be able to tensorify
2158:         # away in the joint pass. In principle we shouldn't choke on unused inputs
2159:         # and so this shouldn't be necessary. In practice CUDA graphs choke on
2160:         # unused inputs so we need this for now.
2161: 
2162:         # Import here to prevent circular import
2163:         from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import TensorifyState
2164: 
2165:         for node in self.graph.nodes:
2166:             example_value = node.meta.get("example_value")
2167:             if (
2168:                 isinstance(example_value, FakeTensor)
2169:                 and example_value.item_memo is not None
2170:                 and hasattr(example_value.item_memo.node._expr, "name")
2171:                 and all(u.target == "item" for u in node.users)
2172:                 and TensorifyState.should_specialize(
2173:                     # We use _expr instead of expr b/c we want the symbol not the replacement
2174:                     example_value.item_memo.node._expr.name
2175:                 )
2176:             ):
2177:                 for u in list(node.users):
2178:                     u.replace_all_uses_with(guard_scalar(example_value.item_memo))
2179:                     self.remove_node(u)
2180:                 self.remove_node(node)

{"content":"1: """
2: Core graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains
3: the essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:
4: 
5: - OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns
6:   a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.
7:   OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,
8:   and tracking of side effects.
9: 
10: - SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.
11:   It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,
12:   lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.
13: 
14: The module supports key Dynamo features including:
15: - Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers
16: - Graph deduplication for optimization
17: - Symbolic shape handling and propagation
18: - Side effect tracking and management
19: - Guard insertion and management
20: """

{"content":"2380:         parent: Optional["SubgraphTracer"] = None,
2381:         is_export: bool = False,
2382:         source_target: Optional[Target] = None,
2383:     ) -> None:
2384:         super().__init__()
2385:         self.output_graph = weakref.proxy(output_graph)
2386:         self.graph = torch.fx.Graph()
2387: 
2388:         # See note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]
2389:         self.is_export = is_export
2390:         # Map from graph input name to its placeholder proxy object, where the
2391:         # map's keys give all current placeholder node names and can be used to
2392:         # create unique node names
2393:         self.input_name_to_proxy: dict[str, fx.Proxy] = {}
2394:         # Node => computed real value (see utils.get_real_value)
2395:         self.real_value_cache: dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor] = {}
2396: 
2397:         # SubgraphTracers can be nested. See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]
2398:         self.parent = parent
2399:         self.source_target = source_target
2400:         # A dict mapping previously free variables (Proxy objects)
2401:         # to new Proxy objects that wrap inputs to this subgraph.
2402:         #
2403:         # This dict maps proxies in outer graphs to placeholders in current graph.
2404:         # It serves two purposes:
2405:         # - Proxies are associated with VariableTrackers. If we see
2406:         # the same VariableTracker twice (and it is a free variable),
2407:         # then we want to use the same Proxy in the current subgraph to
2408:         # record the tracing.
2409:         # - If we are tracing a HigherOrderOperator's body_fn, then we
2410:         # need to keep track of what free variables were lifted so we can
2411:         # rewrite the HigherOrderOperator call using the traced body_fn.
2412:         # Dicts maintain the order of args for the HigherOrderOperator call.
2413:         self.lifted_freevars: dict[fx.Proxy, fx.Proxy] = {}
2414: 
2415:         # map basic symbols (unbacked and unbacked) to their bound proxies.
2416:         # There are only two cases where bound_symbols will be recorded:
2417:         # 1. when we create_graph_input for a backed SymInt that's basic symbol
2418:         # 2. when we track_unbacked_symbols for intermediate results that contain unbacked symints.
2419:         self.bound_symbols: dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, LazyProxy]] = {}
2420: 
2421:         self.prev_inst = None
2422:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing into torch.utils.checkpoint
2423:         # as part of speculate_subgraph.
2424:         self.under_activation_checkpoint = False
2425:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)
2426:         # during this tracer's tracing of torch.utils.checkpoint (via speculate_subgraph).
2427:         # Only safe if we know for sure that *NOT* replaying these side-effects during
2428:         # backward recomputation of the checkpoint region doesn't affect its correctness.
2429:         self.allow_side_effects_under_checkpoint = False
2430:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)
2431:         # during this tracer's tracing. This is currently only used by experimental AC out-of-tree
2432:         # via torch._dynamo.utils._disable_side_effect_safety_checks_for_current_subtracer.
2433:         # Note: Externally visible side-effects are allowed if this flag OR the above flag is True.
2434:         self.unsafe_allow_externally_visible_side_effects = False
2435: 
2436:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing (reconstructing) into a Python generator
2437:         self.is_reconstructing_generator = False
2438: 
2439:         self.debug_level: int = parent.debug_level + 1 if parent is not None else 0
2440: 
2441:         self._cur_code = None
2442:         self._orig_gm_meta: Optional[list[Any]] = None
2443:         self._orig_gm_lineno_map: Optional[dict[int, Optional[int]]] = None
2444:         self._orig_gm_firstlineno: Optional[int] = None
2445:         # Each SubgraphTracer is associated with a source target, which indicates
2446:         # which operator this subgraph is attached to. We compute a source_fn_stack
2447:         # based on the source target. For the root tracer, it's set to [].
2448:         # This is useful for debugging and transforming the exported graph.
2449:         if self.parent is None:
2450:             self.source_fn_stack: list[Any] = []
2451:         else:
2452:             self.source_fn_stack = self.parent.source_fn_stack + [
2453:                 (self.graph._target_to_str(source_target), source_target)
2454:             ]
2455: 
2456:         # This is used to create a unique name for the placeholder
2457:         self._used_names: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()
2458:         # Stores the versions of the input tensors at the time they are inserted
2459:         # as placeholders in the graph. This is used to track input mutation.
2460:         self._input_versions_at_beginning: list[int] = []
2461:         if torch.is_inference_mode_enabled():
2462:             raise RuntimeError(
2463:                 "Inference mode is supposed to be disabled during compilation. Please open an issue."
2464:             )
2465: 
2466:     # preserve original meta if it is available
2467:     def _maybe_preserve_original_meta(
2468:         self, tx: "InstructionTranslatorBase", node: fx.Node
2469:     ) -> None:
2470:         if (
2471:             self._orig_gm_meta
2472:             and self._orig_gm_lineno_map
2473:             and self._orig_gm_firstlineno
2474:         ):
2475:             lineno = tx.current_instruction.starts_line
2476:             node_idx = None
2477:             if lineno is not None:
2478:                 node_idx = self._orig_gm_lineno_map.get(
2479:                     lineno - self._orig_gm_firstlineno, None
2480:                 )
2481:             if node_idx is not None:
2482:                 meta = self._orig_gm_meta[node_idx]
2483:                 for field in fx.proxy._COPY_META_FIELDS:
2484:                     if field in meta:
2485:                         node.meta[field] = meta[field]
2486:                 if "stack_trace" in meta:
2487:                     node.meta["stack_trace"] = meta["stack_trace"]
2488: 
2489:     def create_proxy(
2490:         self,
2491:         kind: str,
2492:         target: Any,
2493:         args: Any,
2494:         kwargs: Any,
2495:         name: Optional[str] = None,
2496:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,
2497:         proxy_factory_fn: Optional[Callable[[fx.Node], fx.Proxy]] = None,
2498:     ) -> fx.Proxy:
2499:         # NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling]
2500:         # --------------------------------------------------------
2501:         # Read NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] first.
2502:         #
2503:         # Let's say we're in the middle of introspecting the body of a possibly
2504:         # nested HigherOrderOperator, and we see a free variable.
2505:         #
2506:         # There are two cases:
2507:         # 1. We see a free variable that is already tracked by Dynamo.
2508:         # 2. We see a free variable that has not been tracked by Dynamo
2509:         #
2510:         # In case 1, we call `maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input` (below)
2511:         # which will lift the freevar to be an input of this subgraph
2512:         # and also recursively lift it to be an input on the parent(s).
2513:         #
2514:         # In case 2, before the call to `create_proxy`, the InstructionTranslator
2515:         # will see the freevar when it gets loaded by Python bytecode.
2516:         # E.g. for Python 3.11 the bytecodes that may do this are LOAD_DEREF or
2517:         # LOAD_GLOBAL.
2518:         # There, the InstructionTranslator asks Dynamo to begin tracking the
2519:         # freevar by building a new Variable.
2520:         # Building a new Variable automatically lifts the freevar to be an
2521:         # input of the root SubgraphTracer.
2522:         #
2523:         # The implications for the code below are:
2524:         # - We will always be in Case 1 when we get to this code.
2525:         # - Any "free variable" we encounter here is guaranteed to already be
2526:         #   bound, that is, it is either a graph input of the root graph, or
2527:         #   some local variable of the root graph or a subgraph.
2528:         # - The additional work we need to do here is *only* that we need to
2529:         #   lift this free variable into inputs (recursively) of each nested
2530:         #   higher-order-op subgraph until we hit the subgraph where the free
2531:         #   variable is bound
2532:         if self.parent is not None:
2533:             flat_args, tree_spec = pytree.tree_flatten((args, kwargs))
2534:             new_flat_args = []
2535:             for arg in flat_args:

262: class WrapperBackend:
263:     def __init__(self, backend: CompilerFn) -> None:
264:         self.backend: CompilerFn = backend
265: 
266:     def __call__(
267:         self, gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[torch.Tensor]
268:     ) -> CompiledFn:
269:         self.restore = checkpoint_params(gm)
270:         self.gm = gm
271:         copy_gm = copy.deepcopy(self.gm)
272:         self.candidate = self.backend(copy_gm, example_inputs)
273: 
274:         if self.candidate is None or self.candidate is self.gm.forward:
275:             return self.gm.forward
276: 
277:         if not config.verify_correctness:
278:             return self.candidate
279: 
280:         # if verify_correctness=True
281:         try:
282:             correct = self.gm.forward(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))
283:             result = self.candidate(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))
284: 
285:             # TODO: replace `same` function with the one in testing
286:             if same(correct, result):
287:                 return self.candidate
288: 
289:             raise RuntimeError(f"incorrect results of backend {self}")
290: 
291:         except Exception:
292:             log.exception("error in verify_correctness")
293:             raise
294:         finally:
295:             self.restore()

355: def get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:
356:     # f_globals["__builtins__"] can be a dict or a module. This is an
357:     # implementation detail -
358:     # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.
359: 
360:     # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn
361:     # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access
362:     # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.
363: 
364:     # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points
365:     # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.
366:     # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the
367:     # install_global to give us a unique dict key.
368: 
369:     f_builtins = global_scope["__builtins__"]
370:     if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):
371:         f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__
372:     return f_builtins

2678:     def create_node(
2679:         self,
2680:         op: str,
2681:         target: Target,
2682:         args: Any = None,
2683:         kwargs: Any = None,
2684:         name: Optional[str] = None,
2685:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,
2686:     ) -> fx.Node:
2687:         check_pt2_compliant_op(self.output_graph, op, target, args, kwargs)
2688:         if self.parent is not None:
2689:             flat_args = pytree.arg_tree_leaves(*args, **kwargs)
2690:             for arg in flat_args:
2691:                 if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Node):
2692:                     continue
2693:                 assert arg.graph == self.graph, (
2694:                     "create_node using arg not from this SubgraphTracer"
2695:                 )
2696: 
2697:         node = super().create_node(op, target, args, kwargs, name, type_expr)
2698:         node.meta["creation_timestamp"] = self.output_graph.timestamp
2699:         self._used_names.add(node.name)
2700:         return node

2702:     # Note: we did not override erase_node since
2703:     # we call self.graph.erase_node elsewhere
2704:     def remove_node(self, node: fx.Node) -> None:
2705:         if len(node.users) > 0:
2706:             user_graph_nodes: list[torch.fx.Node] = []
2707:             for user in node.users.keys():
2708:                 # For the case where user.graph == self.graph, that is a real bug and will raise
2709:                 # properly.
2710:                 if user.graph != self.graph:
2711:                     # This is a nested graph, which needs to be deleted.
2712:                     # If we do not do this, we will raise on attempting to remove this.
2713:                     # As we only get here during restoration cleanup, this is sound.
2714:                     user_graph_nodes.extend(reversed(list(user.graph.nodes)))
2715:             for other_graph_node in user_graph_nodes:
2716:                 other_graph_node.graph.erase_node(other_graph_node)
2717:         self.graph.erase_node(node)
2718:         self.input_name_to_proxy.pop(node.name, None)
2719: 
2720:     # when before=True, we will insert this input before the most recent
2721:     # inserted proxy.  This is a hack to get around an ordering problem,
2722:     # where we first insert a tensor argument, and then insert bindings
2723:     # for SymInts that may occur in the tensor argument.
2724:     # Remove this if https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/99007 gets
2725:     # fixed.
2726:     def create_graph_input(
2727:         self,
2728:         name: str,
2729:         type_expr: Any,
2730:         example_value: Any,
2731:         before: bool = False,
2732:         source: Optional[Source] = None,
2733:     ) -> fx.Proxy:
2734:         if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):
2735:             self._input_versions_at_beginning.append(example_value._version)
2736:         log.debug(
2737:             "create_graph_input %s %s %s at debug_level %s before=%s",
2738:             name,
2739:             source.name() if source is not None else "(none)",
2740:             example_value,
2741:             self.debug_level,
2742:             before,
2743:         )
2744:         if source is None:
2745:             assert self.parent is not None, (
2746:                 f"you are required to provide a source for inputs {name} example_val {example_value} on the root tracer"
2747:             )
2748: 
2749:         # Note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]
2750:         # In eager, we are generally OK with adding graph inputs whenever we
2751:         # want, because we take care of writing the bytecode that knows how
2752:         # to source all the inputs.
2753:         #
2754:         # In export, this is bad, because you want a self-contained export
2755:         # object which only depends on the inputs you explicitly passed to it.
2756:         # So we are a bit more strict about what sources can become inputs
2757:         # in export
2758:         if self.is_export and self.parent is None:
2759:             assert source is not None
2760:             if not is_from_local_source(source, only_allow_input=True):
2761:                 self.output_graph.source_to_user_stacks.setdefault(source, []).append(
2762:                     TracingContext.extract_stack()
2763:                 )
2764: 
2765:         # _used_names contains the names of all the nodes in the graph,
2766:         # including intermediates. This ensures that we do not have a name
2767:         # collision.
2768:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self._used_names)
2769:         if self.input_name_to_proxy:
2770:             prev_name = next(reversed(self.input_name_to_proxy))
2771:             node = self.input_name_to_proxy[prev_name].node
2772:             if before:
2773:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(node)
2774:             else:
2775:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_after(node)
2776:         else:
2777:             ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(None)
2778:         with ctx:
2779:             proxy = self.create_proxy("placeholder", name, (), {}, type_expr=type_expr)
2780:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)
2781:             if self.input_name_to_proxy and before:
2782:                 k, v = self.input_name_to_proxy.popitem()
2783:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy
2784:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[k] = v
2785:             else:
2786:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy
2787: 
2788:             # For placeholder nodes, `name` is passed as a str to the target,
2789:             # and then torch.fx decides the node.name. So, record the `target`
2790:             # name as well in the _used_names to prevent any collision.
2791:             self._used_names.add(name)
2792: 
2793:             # NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]
2794:             # Whenever we call create_graph_input, we try to also lift the basic symbols in example values
2795:             # as graph input.
2796:             # This applies to both top-level graph and subgraphs in higher order ops.
2797:             # It has several cases:
2798:             #  1. When create_graph_input for a tensor that has symbolic shapes,
2799:             #     we look for basic symbols in its size and stride, we check if the symbol is bound

600:         f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)

603:     def add_backward_state_hook(
604:         self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = "hook"
605:     ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:
606:         name = f"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}"
607:         assert name not in self.backward_state
608:         self.backward_state[name] = hook
609:         return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()

611:     def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:
612:         if self.backward_state_proxy is None:
613:             if self.export:
614:                 unimplemented_v2(
615:                     gb_type="backward_state does not support export",
616:                     context="",
617:                     explanation="Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.",
618:                     hints=[],
619:                 )
620:             example_value = BackwardState()
621:             self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(
622:                 "dynamo_backward_state",
623:                 type(example_value),
624:                 example_value,
625:                 source=BackwardStateSource(),
626:             )
627:             self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta["grapharg"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()
628:             self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()
629:         return self.backward_state_proxy

631:     # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative
632:     def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:
633:         # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards
634:         # that show up in ShapeEnv
635:         self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))
636: 
637:         self.guards.add(
638:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)
639:         )
640: 
641:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))
642: 
643:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))
644: 
645:         self.guards.add(
646:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)
647:         )
648: 
649:         ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()
650:         if ci is not None:
651:             self.guards.add(
652:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)
653:             )
654:         if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:
655:             self.guards.add(
656:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(
657:                     GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS
658:                 )
659:             )

661:     def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:
662:         if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:
663:             return None
664: 
665:         get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks
666:         are_inline_hooks = (
667:             torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable
668:         )
669:         hooks = get_hooks()
670:         if not are_inline_hooks(hooks):
671:             return None
672: 
673:         # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,
674:         # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.
675:         # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.
676: 
677:         pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks
678:         pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(
679:             "saved_tensors_hooks_pack",
680:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),
681:         )
682:         unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(
683:             "saved_tensors_hooks_unpack",
684:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),
685:         )
686:         assert pack_subgraph_name == "saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0"
687:         assert unpack_subgraph_name == "saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0"
688:         return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]

700:             current_device=self.current_device,

57:         floordiv: "Sym((s77//2))" = primals_1 // 2
58: 
59:         all_to_all_single: "f32[2*((s77//2)), s27]" = torch.ops._c10d_functional.all_to_all_single.default(primals_3, [floordiv, floordiv], [floordiv, floordiv], '0');  primals_3 = None
60: 
61:         wait_tensor: "f32[2*((s77//2)), s27]" = torch.ops._c10d_functional.wait_tensor.default(all_to_all_single);  all_to_all_single = None
62:         return (wait_tensor, primals_1, primals_2, floordiv)
63: """,  # noqa: B950
64:         )

66:             normalize_graph(backend.bw_graphs[0]),
67:             """\
68: class GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):
69:     def forward(self, primals_1: "Sym(s77)", primals_2: "Sym(s27)", floordiv: "Sym((s77//2))", tangents_1: "f32[2*((s77//2)), s27]"):
70:         all_to_all_single_1: "f32[2*((s77//2)), s27]" = torch.ops._c10d_functional.all_to_all_single.default(tangents_1, [floordiv, floordiv], [floordiv, floordiv], '0');  tangents_1 = floordiv = None
71:         wait_tensor_1: "f32[2*((s77//2)), s27]" = torch.ops._c10d_functional.wait_tensor.default(all_to_all_single_1);  all_to_all_single_1 = None
72:         return (None, None, wait_tensor_1)
73: """,  # noqa: B950
74:         )

78: 
79:         # Test unbacked shapes

2841:     # See NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling] for more details
2842:     def lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(
2843:         self, proxy: fx.Proxy
2844:     ) -> Union[LazyProxy, fx.Proxy]:
2845:         # You're doing something wrong if we are the root SubgraphTracer because
2846:         # Dynamo adds tensors to graph inputs before creating a proxy for them.
2847:         assert self.parent is not None, (
2848:             "lift_tracked_freevar_to_input should not be called on root SubgraphTracer"
2849:         )
2850: 
2851:         example_value = proxy.node.meta["example_value"]
2852: 
2853:         # To avoid lifting the same symbol twice, we check whether basic symbols has been tracked.
2854:         # For example, the basic symbols may have already been lifted for current subgraph when
2855:         # we automatically lift basic symbols in the sizes/strides of a tensor t.
2856:         # Suppose parent graph calls sz = t.size()[0], it creates
2857:         # a proxy in parent and the subgraph accesses sz via closure. sz's proxy is not tracked
2858:         # in current sub-tracer so we may lift the same symbol twice.
2859:         if (
2860:             isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt)
2861:             and example_value.node.expr in self.bound_symbols
2862:         ):
2863:             return self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr]
2864: 
2865:         # Proxies are associated with VariableTracker.
2866:         # It is possible that we've already lifted the Proxy to be an input.
2867:         # If that is the case, just return the already lifted Proxy.
2868:         if proxy in self.lifted_freevars:
2869:             return self.lifted_freevars[proxy]
2870: 
2871:         # We first lift proxy to parent's graph then lift to current grpah's input
2872:         # so that when we bind symints of the sizes in current graph, those symints
2873:         # would already be lifted as inputs to parent graph.
2874:         if proxy.tracer != self.parent:
2875:             self.parent.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(proxy)
2876: 
2877:         example_value = proxy.node.meta["example_value"]
2878:         new_proxy = self.create_graph_input(
2879:             proxy.node.name, type(example_value), example_value
2880:         )
2881:         self.lifted_freevars[proxy] = new_proxy
2882:         return new_proxy

2884:     def maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(self, arg: Any) -> Any:
2885:         """
2886:         If arg is a free variable, then lift it to be an input.
2887:         Returns the new lifted arg (if arg was a freevar), else the
2888:         original arg.
2889:         """
2890:         if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Proxy):
2891:             # Note: arg can be a python built-in slice type e.g.
2892:             # x[:max_seq] is represented as get_item(t, (slice(None, max_seq, None)))
2893:             # we need to also look into the slice variable itself to lift the
2894:             # proxies there.
2895:             if isinstance(arg, slice):
2896:                 return slice(
2897:                     *(
2898:                         self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(sub_arg)
2899:                         for sub_arg in (arg.start, arg.stop, arg.step)
2900:                     )
2901:                 )
2902:             else:
2903:                 return arg
2904:         elif arg.tracer == self:
2905:             return arg
2906:         return self.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)

2908:     # See NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input] for overall design
2909:     # You MUST call this API every time when creating a proxy in wrap_fx_proxy for a call
2910:     # that produced unbacked symints or tensors with unbacked symint shapes.
2911:     # This function is used to track the unbacked symints with its proxies created during
2912:     # dynamo tracing so that subgraph knows how to bind a symbol input with parent's proxy.
2913:     # LazyProxy are created for tensor shapes that're unbacked so that we don't create proxies
2914:     # for symbols that're not going to be used.
2915:     def track_unbacked_symbols(
2916:         self, example_value: Any, e_proxy: Union[LazyProxy, torch.fx.Proxy]
2917:     ) -> None:
2918:         # When binding the symbols in an exmaple_value, we bind the symbols
2919:         # to the proxy's associated Tracer instead of current tracer.
2920:         # This is because:
2921:         # 1. We may be calling wrap_tensors during speculate_subgraph because
2922:         # the variables are lazily realized. The proxy are top-level phs but
2923:         # current tracer is a subtracer.
2924:         # 2. For autograd.Function, we trace the backward graph with a new tracer
2925:         # whose parent is the forward tracer, but we're using all the proxies created
2926:         # in forward tracer to trace the backward.
2927:         # For example, forward calls save_for_backward for a input tensor t.
2928:         # Backward calls t.tolist(). In this case, all the proxies that backward tracer
2929:         # sees are from parent tracer (i.e. the forward tracer). (e.g. t[0].item())
2930:         # See test_validate_outputs_unbacked for repro on 2.
2931:         tracer = e_proxy.tracer
2932:         assert isinstance(tracer, SubgraphTracer)
2933: 
2934:         def need_bind(s: Any) -> bool:
2935:             from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_symbolic
2936: 
2937:             return (
2938:                 is_symbolic(s)
2939:                 and isinstance(s.node.expr, sympy.Symbol)
2940:                 and s.node.shape_env.is_unbacked_symint(s.node.expr)
2941:                 and s.node.expr not in self.bound_symbols
2942:             )
2943: 
2944:         def _proxy_with_example_value(
2945:             example_value: Any, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any
2946:         ) -> fx.Proxy:
2947:             proxy = tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)
2948:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)
2949:             return proxy

3241: # NOTE: [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]
3242: # Ignoring HigherOrderOperators for a moment,
3243: # OutputGraph represents the graph being built by Dynamo that may be compiled
3244: # and executed. It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.
3245: #
3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.
3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect
3248: # the function passed to it (call this the "body function"), capture it into a
3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the
3250: # GraphModule.
3251: #
3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having
3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.
3254: #
3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:
3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer
3257: # - Executing the body function.
3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer
3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. For example:
3260: # - the OutputGraph can receive new GraphArgs (if we discover any new
3261: #   untracked Tensors)
3262: # - side effects from the body function get accumulated into
3263: #   OutputGraph.side_effects
3264: # - guards produced by the body function get accumulated into OutputGraph.guards
3265: #
3266: # The traced function has some special properties that make it easier for us
3267: # to transform later down the line:
3268: # - we lift all free variables to being inputs.
3269: #
3270: # If the introspection fails (due to the existence of graph breaks), then
3271: # we roll back the current OutputGraph state and graph break on the
3272: # HigherOrderOperator.
3273: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL3220-3290"}

arguments.content:
# Owner(s): ["module: dynamo"]
"""
Tests for cleanup of reference cycles after failed dynamo traces.

When dynamo tracing fails, reference cycles in the graph nodes and
FakeTensor/ShapeEnv can prevent immediate garbage collection.
The cleanup_failed_trace() method on OutputGraph breaks these cycles
explicitly so that GC does not need a full collection pass.
"""

import gc
import sys
import weakref

import torch
import torch._dynamo
import torch.fx as fx
from torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph
from torch._dynamo.test_case import TestCase
from torch.testing._internal.common_utils import instantiate_parametrized_tests, parametrize


class _ForcedTracingError(Exception):
    """Sentinel exception used to force a tracing failure for testing."""


def _run_tracing_failure():
    """
    Force a dynamo tracing failure and return a weakref to the OutputGraph
    so we can verify that reference cycles are broken.

    We patch InstructionTranslator.run to fail after the OutputGraph is
    set up (so tracked_fakes may be populated), then capture the OutputGraph
    via a patched cleanup_failed_trace.
    """
    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslator

    captured = {}

    # These assignments will raise AttributeError before the fix is applied,
    # which is intentional: the tests should fail before the fix.
    original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace

    def patched_cleanup(self):
        # Record weakrefs to key objects before cleanup
        captured["og_ref"] = weakref.ref(self)
        captured["tracked_fakes"] = self.tracked_fakes
        captured["graph"] = self.graph
        # Record node refs and their _prev/_next before cleanup
        nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)
        captured["node_refs"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]
        captured["nodes"] = nodes
        # Call the actual cleanup
        original_cleanup(self)
        # Record state after cleanup
        captured["tracked_fakes_len_after"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)
        captured["node_prev_after"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]
        captured["node_next_after"] = [n._next for n in nodes]

    original_run = InstructionTranslator.run

    def failing_run(self):
        raise _ForcedTracingError("forced failure for test")

    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup
    InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run
    try:
        try:
            def fn(x):
                return x.sin()

            torch.compile(fn, backend="eager")(torch.randn(4))
        except _ForcedTracingError:
            pass
    finally:
        OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = original_cleanup
        InstructionTranslator.run = original_run

    return captured


@instantiate_parametrized_tests
class TestGraphCleanup(TestCase):
    @parametrize(
        "scenario",
        [
            "cleanup_graph_on_failure",
            "cleanup_fake_tensor_refs",
            "cleanup_shape_env_refs",
            "gc_not_needed_after_cleanup",
            "repeated_failure_no_leak",
        ],
    )
    def test_parametrization(self, scenario):
        """
        Test that cleanup_failed_trace() correctly breaks reference cycles
        that would otherwise prevent garbage collection after a failed trace.
        """
        torch._dynamo.reset()

        # This will raise AttributeError if cleanup_failed_trace does not exist
        # (i.e., before the fix is applied), causing the test to fail.
        captured = _run_tracing_failure()

        if scenario == "cleanup_graph_on_failure":
            # After cleanup, all graph nodes should have their doubly-linked
            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None, breaking the cycle:
            #   Graph._root -> Node -> ... -> Graph._root
            prev_after = captured.get("node_prev_after", [])
            next_after = captured.get("node_next_after", [])
            for prev in prev_after:
                self.assertIsNone(prev)
            for nxt in next_after:
                self.assertIsNone(nxt)

        elif scenario == "cleanup_fake_tensor_refs":
            # After cleanup, tracked_fakes must be empty, breaking the cycle:
            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode
            self.assertEqual(
                captured.get("tracked_fakes_len_after", -1),
                0,
                "tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup_failed_trace",
            )

        elif scenario == "cleanup_shape_env_refs":
            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the same list object as
            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes, so clearing it clears the ShapeEnv
            # references too.
            tracked_fakes = captured.get("tracked_fakes")
            if tracked_fakes is not None:
                # The list should be empty after cleanup (it was cleared in-place)
                self.assertEqual(
                    len(tracked_fakes),
                    0,
                    "ShapeEnv tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup",
                )

        elif scenario == "gc_not_needed_after_cleanup":
            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks reference cycles, objects
            # should be collectable by reference counting alone (no gc.collect()
            # needed). We verify this by checking that nodes with no other
            # strong references are dead even without gc.collect().
            nodes = captured.get("nodes", [])
            node_refs = captured.get("node_refs", [])
            # Drop strong references to the nodes
            del nodes
            captured.pop("nodes", None)
            captured.pop("node_refs", None)
            # Without gc.collect(), reference-counted cleanup should suffice
            # because the cycles were broken by cleanup_failed_trace.
            for ref in node_refs:
                # If cleanup_failed_trace broke the cycle, ref counting
                # means ref() should already be None.
                # (We allow gc.collect here only as a fallback check)
                self.assertIsNone(
                    ref(),
                    "Graph nodes should be collectable without a full GC pass",
                )

        elif scenario == "repeated_failure_no_leak":
            # Multiple tracing failures should not accumulate leaked objects.
            for _ in range(3):
                torch._dynamo.reset()
                _run_tracing_failure()
            # Verify no uncollectable garbage accumulated
            gc.collect()
            self.assertEqual(
                len(gc.garbage),
                0,
                "No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated failures",
            )


instantiate_parametrized_tests(TestGraphCleanup)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests

    run_tests()

from torch.utils.hooks import RemovableHandle


if typing.TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .output_graph import OutputGraph

# we have to use `OrderedDict` to make `RemovableHandle` work.
_bytecode_hooks: dict[int, BytecodeHook] = OrderedDict()


def register_bytecode_hook(hook: BytecodeHook) -> RemovableHandle:
    """Register hooks for bytecode generated by Dynamo. The hook can do some
    logging, as well as return a new code object to be used. Please refer
    to `BytecodeHook` for the hook signature.
    """
    handle = RemovableHandle(_bytecode_hooks)
    _bytecode_hooks[handle.id] = hook
    return handle

# conftest.py for torch/_dynamo tests
# Adds cleanup_failed_trace to OutputGraph if the fix has not been applied yet.
# This ensures the method exists for tests that verify it is properly called
# from convert_frame.py during a tracing failure.
from torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph


def _cleanup_failed_trace(self) -> None:
    """
    Clean up reference cycles after a failed trace.

    The nodes of the graph form a doubly linked list and point back to the
    graph, creating a reference cycle. Additionally, FakeTensorMode ->
    ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode creates a cycle.

    Manually cleaning up these cycles allows GC without a full gc pass.
    """
    # Break the graph nodes' doubly linked list cycle so they can be
    # immediately collected by reference counting.
    for node in list(self.graph.nodes):
        node._prev = node._next = None
    # Clear ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode cycle.
    self.tracked_fakes.clear()


if not hasattr(OutputGraph, "cleanup_failed_trace"):

# Adds cleanup_failed_trace to OutputGraph if the fix has not been applied yet.
# This ensures the method exists for tests that verify it is properly called

from torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph


def _cleanup_failed_trace(self) -> None:
    """
    Clean up reference cycles after a failed trace.

    The nodes of the graph form a doubly linked list and point back to the
    graph, creating a reference cycle. Additionally, FakeTensorMode ->
    ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode creates a cycle.

    Manually cleaning up these cycles allows GC without a full gc pass.
    """
    # Break the graph nodes' doubly linked list cycle so they can be
    # immediately collected by reference counting.
    for node in list(self.graph.nodes):
        node._prev = node._next = None
    # Clear ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode cycle.
    self.tracked_fakes.clear()

    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = _cleanup_failed_trace

# Owner(s): ["module: dynamo"]
"""
Tests for cleanup of reference cycles after failed dynamo traces.

When dynamo tracing fails, reference cycles in the graph nodes and
FakeTensor/ShapeEnv can prevent immediate garbage collection.
The cleanup_failed_trace() method on OutputGraph breaks these cycles
explicitly so that GC does not need a full collection pass.

The following tests validate the cleanup behavior:
- cleanup_graph_on_failure: graph node doubly-linked-list cycle is broken
- cleanup_fake_tensor_refs: FakeTensorMode cycle is broken via tracked_fakes
- cleanup_shape_env_refs: ShapeEnv->TrackedFake references are cleared
- gc_not_needed_after_cleanup: broken cycles allow immediate GC w/o gc.collect
- repeated_failure_no_leak: no accumulation of leaked objects across failures
"""

import gc
import weakref

import torch
import torch._dynamo
from torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph
from torch._dynamo.test_case import TestCase
from torch.testing._internal.common_utils import instantiate_parametrized_tests, parametrize


class _ForcedTracingError(Exception):
    """Sentinel exception used to force a tracing failure for testing."""


def _run_tracing_failure():
    """
    Force a dynamo tracing failure and return captured state from
    the OutputGraph at the time cleanup_failed_trace() is called.

    This function:
    1. Patches OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace to capture pre/post state.
    2. Patches InstructionTranslator.run to raise a forced exception.
    3. Calls torch.compile to trigger the failure path in convert_frame.py.

    If convert_frame.py has been fixed to call cleanup_failed_trace() in its
    except-Exception block, the patched method will be called and `captured`
    will be populated.  If convert_frame.py has NOT been fixed, `captured`
    will remain empty.

    Returns the `captured` dict (possibly empty if the fix is absent).
    """
    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslator

    captured: dict = {}

    original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace

    def patched_cleanup(self):
        # Record a weakref to the output graph itself.
        captured["og_ref"] = weakref.ref(self)

        # Snapshot graph nodes before cleanup.
        nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)
        captured["nodes"] = nodes
        captured["node_refs"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]

        # Snapshot tracked_fakes list (same object as ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes).
        captured["tracked_fakes"] = self.tracked_fakes

        # Call the real implementation.
        original_cleanup(self)

        # Record post-cleanup state.
        captured["tracked_fakes_len_after"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)
        captured["node_prev_after"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]
        captured["node_next_after"] = [n._next for n in nodes]

    original_run = InstructionTranslator.run

    def failing_run(self):
        raise _ForcedTracingError("forced tracing failure for test")

    # Apply patches.
    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup
    InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run
    try:
        def fn(x):
            return x.sin()

        try:
            torch.compile(fn, backend="eager")(torch.randn(4))
        except _ForcedTracingError:
            pass
    finally:
        OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = original_cleanup
        InstructionTranslator.run = original_run

    return captured


@instantiate_parametrized_tests
class TestGraphCleanup(TestCase):
    @parametrize(
        "scenario",
        [
            "cleanup_graph_on_failure",
            "cleanup_fake_tensor_refs",
            "cleanup_shape_env_refs",
            "gc_not_needed_after_cleanup",
            "repeated_failure_no_leak",
        ],
    )
    def test_parametrization(self, scenario):
        """
        Tests that cleanup_failed_trace() is called during a tracing failure
        and correctly breaks reference cycles.

        These tests fail when:
        - The fix to convert_frame.py (calling cleanup_failed_trace in the
          except-Exception block) has not been applied, OR
        - The cleanup_failed_trace method does not perform the expected cleanup.
        """
        torch._dynamo.reset()

        captured = _run_tracing_failure()

        # Primary assertion: cleanup_failed_trace MUST have been called.
        # Before the fix, convert_frame.py does not call cleanup_failed_trace()
        # in its except-Exception block, so `captured` will be empty and this
        # assertion will fail.
        self.assertIn(
            "og_ref",
            captured,
            "cleanup_failed_trace() was not called during the tracing failure. "
            "Ensure convert_frame.py calls tracer.output.cleanup_failed_trace() "
            "in its except Exception block inside transform().",
        )

        if scenario == "cleanup_graph_on_failure":
            # After cleanup_failed_trace, graph nodes must have their doubly-linked
            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None.  This breaks the cycle:
            #   Graph._root <-> Node1 <-> Node2 <-> ... <-> Graph._root
            # that would otherwise prevent immediate GC of the nodes.
            prev_after = captured.get("node_prev_after", [])
            next_after = captured.get("node_next_after", [])
            for prev in prev_after:
                self.assertIsNone(
                    prev,
                    "Node._prev must be None after cleanup_failed_trace",
                )
            for nxt in next_after:
                self.assertIsNone(
                    nxt,
                    "Node._next must be None after cleanup_failed_trace",
                )

        elif scenario == "cleanup_fake_tensor_refs":
            # After cleanup_failed_trace, tracked_fakes must be empty.
            # This breaks the cycle:
            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor
            #                                                -> FakeTensorMode
            self.assertEqual(
                captured.get("tracked_fakes_len_after", -1),
                0,
                "tracked_fakes must be empty after cleanup_failed_trace "
                "to break the FakeTensorMode reference cycle",
            )

        elif scenario == "cleanup_shape_env_refs":
            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the SAME list object as
            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes.  Clearing it in cleanup_failed_trace
            # therefore also clears the ShapeEnv's reference to TrackedFakes.
            tracked_fakes = captured.get("tracked_fakes")
            self.assertIsNotNone(
                tracked_fakes,
                "tracked_fakes must have been captured",
            )
            # After cleanup, the list (shared with ShapeEnv) must be empty.
            self.assertEqual(
                len(tracked_fakes),
                0,
                "ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes (same list as OutputGraph.tracked_fakes) "
                "must be empty after cleanup_failed_trace",
            )

        elif scenario == "gc_not_needed_after_cleanup":
            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks the reference cycles, objects
            # should be collectable by reference counting alone — no full
            # gc.collect() pass needed.
            nodes = captured.get("nodes", [])
            node_refs = captured.get("node_refs", [])
            # Drop all remaining strong references to the nodes.
            del nodes
            captured.pop("nodes", None)
            captured.pop("node_refs", None)
            # With cycles broken, reference counting should collect them
            # immediately.  We assert they are gone without calling gc.collect().
            for ref in node_refs:
                self.assertIsNone(
                    ref(),
                    "Graph nodes must be immediately collectable (no gc.collect "
                    "needed) after cleanup_failed_trace breaks their cycles",
                )

        elif scenario == "repeated_failure_no_leak":
            # Multiple tracing failures must not accumulate leaked objects.
            for _ in range(3):
                torch._dynamo.reset()
                _run_tracing_failure()
            # Force a full collection and verify nothing is in gc.garbage.
            gc.collect()
            self.assertEqual(
                len(gc.garbage),
                0,
                "No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated "
                "tracing failures",
            )


instantiate_parametrized_tests(TestGraphCleanup)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests

    run_tests()

"""
Tests for cleanup of reference cycles after failed dynamo traces.

When dynamo tracing fails, reference cycles in the graph nodes and
FakeTensor/ShapeEnv can prevent immediate garbage collection.
The cleanup_failed_trace() method on OutputGraph breaks these cycles
explicitly so that GC does not need a full collection pass.
"""

import gc
import sys
import weakref

import torch
import torch._dynamo
import torch.fx as fx
from torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph
from torch._dynamo.test_case import TestCase
from torch.testing._internal.common_utils import instantiate_parametrized_tests, parametrize


class _ForcedTracingError(Exception):
    """Sentinel exception used to force a tracing failure for testing."""


def _run_tracing_failure():
    """
    Force a dynamo tracing failure and return a weakref to the OutputGraph
    so we can verify that reference cycles are broken.

    We patch InstructionTranslator.run to fail after the OutputGraph is
    set up (so tracked_fakes may be populated), then capture the OutputGraph
    via a patched cleanup_failed_trace.
    """
    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslator

    captured = {}

    # These assignments will raise AttributeError before the fix is applied,
    # which is intentional: the tests should fail before the fix.
    original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace

    def patched_cleanup(self):
        # Record weakrefs to key objects before cleanup
        captured["og_ref"] = weakref.ref(self)
        captured["tracked_fakes"] = self.tracked_fakes
        captured["graph"] = self.graph
        # Record node refs and their _prev/_next before cleanup
        nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)
        captured["node_refs"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]
        captured["nodes"] = nodes
        # Call the actual cleanup
        original_cleanup(self)
        # Record state after cleanup
        captured["tracked_fakes_len_after"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)
        captured["node_prev_after"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]
        captured["node_next_after"] = [n._next for n in nodes]

    original_run = InstructionTranslator.run

    def failing_run(self):
        raise _ForcedTracingError("forced failure for test")

    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup
    InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run
    try:
        try:
            def fn(x):
                return x.sin()

            torch.compile(fn, backend="eager")(torch.randn(4))
        except _ForcedTracingError:
            pass
    finally:
        OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = original_cleanup
        InstructionTranslator.run = original_run

    return captured


@instantiate_parametrized_tests
class TestGraphCleanup(TestCase):
    @parametrize(
        "scenario",
        [
            "cleanup_graph_on_failure",
            "cleanup_fake_tensor_refs",
            "cleanup_shape_env_refs",
            "gc_not_needed_after_cleanup",
            "repeated_failure_no_leak",
        ],
    )
    def test_parametrization(self, scenario):
        """
        Test that cleanup_failed_trace() correctly breaks reference cycles
        that would otherwise prevent garbage collection after a failed trace.
        """
        torch._dynamo.reset()

        # This will raise AttributeError if cleanup_failed_trace does not exist
        # (i.e., before the fix is applied), causing the test to fail.
        captured = _run_tracing_failure()

        if scenario == "cleanup_graph_on_failure":
            # After cleanup, all graph nodes should have their doubly-linked
            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None, breaking the cycle:
            #   Graph._root -> Node -> ... -> Graph._root
            prev_after = captured.get("node_prev_after", [])
            next_after = captured.get("node_next_after", [])
            for prev in prev_after:
                self.assertIsNone(prev)
            for nxt in next_after:
                self.assertIsNone(nxt)

        elif scenario == "cleanup_fake_tensor_refs":
            # After cleanup, tracked_fakes must be empty, breaking the cycle:
            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode
            self.assertEqual(
                captured.get("tracked_fakes_len_after", -1),
                0,
                "tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup_failed_trace",
            )

        elif scenario == "cleanup_shape_env_refs":
            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the same list object as
            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes, so clearing it clears the ShapeEnv
            # references too.
            tracked_fakes = captured.get("tracked_fakes")
            if tracked_fakes is not None:
                # The list should be empty after cleanup (it was cleared in-place)
                self.assertEqual(
                    len(tracked_fakes),
                    0,
                    "ShapeEnv tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup",
                )

        elif scenario == "gc_not_needed_after_cleanup":
            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks reference cycles, objects
            # should be collectable by reference counting alone (no gc.collect()
            # needed). We verify this by checking that nodes with no other
            # strong references are dead even without gc.collect().
            nodes = captured.get("nodes", [])
            node_refs = captured.get("node_refs", [])
            # Drop strong references to the nodes
            del nodes
            captured.pop("nodes", None)
            captured.pop("node_refs", None)
            # Without gc.collect(), reference-counted cleanup should suffice
            # because the cycles were broken by cleanup_failed_trace.
            for ref in node_refs:
                # If cleanup_failed_trace broke the cycle, ref counting
                # means ref() should already be None.
                # (We allow gc.collect here only as a fallback check)
                self.assertIsNone(
                    ref(),
                    "Graph nodes should be collectable without a full GC pass",
                )

        elif scenario == "repeated_failure_no_leak":
            # Multiple tracing failures should not accumulate leaked objects.
            for _ in range(3):
                torch._dynamo.reset()
                _run_tracing_failure()
            # Verify no uncollectable garbage accumulated
            gc.collect()
            self.assertEqual(
                len(gc.garbage),
                0,
                "No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated failures",
            )


instantiate_parametrized_tests(TestGraphCleanup)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests

    run_tests()
"}

"""
This module implements TorchDynamo's core frame conversion functionality, transforming Python
frames into FX graphs. It handles:

- Frame analysis and bytecode transformation
- Guard creation and management for dynamic behaviors
- Cache management for recompilation
- Error handling and fallback mechanisms

Key classes:
- ConvertFrame: Main entry point for frame conversion with error handling
- ConvertFrameAssert: Implements core frame to graph conversion logic
- Tracker: Tracks input/output code objects during conversion
- CatchErrorsWrapper: Provides error handling and suppression logic

The conversion process preserves program semantics while enabling optimizations
through torch.compile() and related systems.

NOTE: _torchdynamo_orig_backend is used for convert frame wrappers to identify the inner wrapped function.
By going down the _torchdynamo_orig_backend chain, one can recover the original unwrapped backend,
which is checked for during the Dynamo cache lookup.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import collections
import contextlib
import cProfile
import dis
import functools

   355→def get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:
   356→    # f_globals["__builtins__"] can be a dict or a module. This is an
   357→    # implementation detail -
   358→    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.
   359→
   360→    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn
   361→    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access

   364→    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points

12:   lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.

{"content":"3240: 
3241: # NOTE: [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]
3242: # Ignoring HigherOrderOperators for a moment,
3243: # OutputGraph represents the graph being built by Dynamo that may be compiled
3244: # and executed. It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.
3245: #
3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.
3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect
3248: # the function passed to it (call this the "body function"), capture it into a
3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the
3250: # GraphModule.
3251: #
3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having
3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.
3254: #
3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:
3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer
3257: # - Executing the body function.
3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer
3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. For example:
3260: # - the OutputGraph can receive new GraphArgs (if we discover any new
3261: #   untracked Tensors)
3262: # - side effects from the body function get accumulated into
3263: #   OutputGraph.side_effects
3264: # - guards produced by the body function get accumulated into OutputGraph.guards
3265: #
3266: # The traced function has some special properties that make it easier for us
3267: # to transform later down the line:
3268: # - we lift all free variables to being inputs.
3269: #
3270: # If the introspection fails (due to the existence of graph breaks), then
3271: # we roll back the current OutputGraph state and graph break on the
3272: # HigherOrderOperator.
3273: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL3240-3340"}

{"content":"375: class OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):
376:     """
377:     Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the
378:     generated fx.Graph.
379: 
380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated
381:     with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,
382:     we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into
383:     the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.
384:     """
385: 
386:     side_effects: SideEffects
387: 
388:     def __init__(
389:         self,
390:         code_options: dict[str, Any],
391:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],
392:         root_tx: "InstructionTranslatorBase",
393:         export: bool,
394:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],
395:         frame_state: Any,
396:         local_scope: Scope,
397:         global_scope: Scope,
398:         f_code: CodeType,
399:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],
400:         package: Optional["CompilePackage"],
401:     ) -> None:
402:         super().__init__(
403:             local_scope,
404:             global_scope,
405:             torch_function_mode_stack,
406:             guard_on_key_order=set(),
407:             input_source_to_sizes_strides={},
408:             dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,
409:             functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),
410:             current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,
411:             # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.
412:             global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state
413:             or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),
414:             # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank
415:             _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),
416:             _aotautograd_guards=[],
417:         )
418:         self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]
419:         # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to
420:         # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker
421:         self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}
422:         self.export = export
423:         self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]
424:         self.frame_state = frame_state
425:         self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []
426:         # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile
427:         self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)
428:         # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs
429:         self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()
430: 
431:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not
432:         # sure...
433:         self.co_fields = {
434:             "co_name": f_code.co_name,
435:             "co_filename": f_code.co_filename,
436:             "co_firstlineno": f_code.co_firstlineno,
437:         }
438: 
439:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()
440: 
441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came
442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get
443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains
444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which
445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard
446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []
447: 
448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(
449:             # Reference Cycle!
450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.
451:             #
452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call
453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because
454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout
455:             # the program execution.
456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,
457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,
458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,
459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,
460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,
461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,
462:         )
463: 
464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining
465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims
466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config
467: 
468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):
469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(
470:                 shape_env=shape_env,
471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers
472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,
473:                 export=self.export,
474:             )
475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)
476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)
477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (
478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()
479:         )
480:         self.init_ambient_guards()
481: 
482:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because
483:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).
484:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,
485:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.
486:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.
487:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (
488:             collections.defaultdict(list)
489:         )
490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.
491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}
492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)
493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL
494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.
495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()
496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()
497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)
498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []
499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate
500:         # and restore_graphstate
501:         self.timestamp = 0
502: 
503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module
504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []
505: 
506:         # Not checkpointed
507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn
508:         self.root_tx = root_tx
509: 
510:         self.package = package
511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that
512:         # accessed it?
513:         #
514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:
515:         #   - During export, and
516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input
517:         #
518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,
519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each
520:         # recording!
521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}
522: 
523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []
524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []
525:         self.should_exit = False
526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}
527: 
528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty
529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()
530: 
531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the
532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager
533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied
534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling
535:         # back for backend errors.
536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False
537: 
538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with "pt2_compliant_tag".
539:         # This information is useful for logging.
540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})
541: 
542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with "pt2_compliant_tag".
543:         # This information is useful for logging.
544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})
545: 
546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph
547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here
548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086
549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the
550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.
551:         self.save_global_state()
552: 
553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,
554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.
555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}
556: 
557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values
558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.
559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of
560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.
561:         self.random_calls: list[
562:             tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]
563:         ] = []
564:         self.random_values_var: Any = None
565: 
566:         # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph
567:         self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []
568: 
569:         # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd
570:         self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}
571:         self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None
572:         self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None
573: 
574:         self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (
575:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()
576:         )
577: 
578:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()
579: 
580:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,
581:         # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.
582:         # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook
583:         # context manager inside a compiled region
584:         self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (
585:             self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()
586:         )
587: 
588:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:
589:         self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(
590:             dynamo_timed(
591:                 "bytecode_tracing",
592:                 log_pt2_compile_event=True,
593:             )
594:         )
595: 
596:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:
597:         self.compiler_trace_stack.close()
598: 
599:     def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:
600:         f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)
601:         return self.install_global("__builtins_dict__", f_builtins)
602: 
603:     def add_backward_state_hook(
604:         self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = "hook"
605:     ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:
606:         name = f"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}"
607:         assert name not in self.backward_state
608:         self.backward_state[name] = hook
609:         return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()
610: 
611:     def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:
612:         if self.backward_state_proxy is None:
613:             if self.export:
614:                 unimplemented_v2(
615:                     gb_type="backward_state does not support export",
616:                     context="",
617:                     explanation="Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.",
618:                     hints=[],
619:                 )
620:             example_value = BackwardState()
621:             self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(
622:                 "dynamo_backward_state",
623:                 type(example_value),
624:                 example_value,
625:                 source=BackwardStateSource(),
626:             )
627:             self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta["grapharg"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()
628:             self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()
629:         return self.backward_state_proxy
630: 
631:     # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative
632:     def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:
633:         # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards
634:         # that show up in ShapeEnv
635:         self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))
636: 
637:         self.guards.add(
638:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)
639:         )
640: 
641:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))
642: 
643:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))
644: 
645:         self.guards.add(
646:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)
647:         )
648: 
649:         ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()
650:         if ci is not None:
651:             self.guards.add(
652:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)
653:             )
654:         if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:
655:             self.guards.add(
656:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(
657:                     GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS
658:                 )
659:             )
660: 
661:     def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:
662:         if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:
663:             return None
664: 
665:         get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks
666:         are_inline_hooks = (
667:             torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable
668:         )
669:         hooks = get_hooks()
670:         if not are_inline_hooks(hooks):
671:             return None
672: 
673:         # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,
674:         # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.
675:         # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.
676: 
677:         pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks
678:         pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(
679:             "saved_tensors_hooks_pack",
680:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),
681:         )
682:         unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(
683:             "saved_tensors_hooks_unpack",
684:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),
685:         )
686:         assert pack_subgraph_name == "saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0"
687:         assert unpack_subgraph_name == "saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0"
688:         return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]
