709:     def synthetic_graph_input(
710:         self, fn: Callable[..., Any], args: tuple[Any, ...]
711:     ) -> VariableTracker:
712:         """
713:         call fn(*args) before the graph runs and turn the result into a fake input.
714:         """
715:         example_value = fn(*args)
716:         varname = self.new_var()
717:         cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)
718:         cg.add_push_null(
719:             lambda: cg.load_import_from(
720:                 fn.__module__,
721:                 fn.__name__,
722:             )
723:         )
724:         cg.foreach(map(variables.ConstantVariable.create, args))
725:         cg.call_function(len(args), False)
726:         cg.store(varname)
727:         self.pregraph_bytecode.extend(cg.get_instructions())
728:         source = SyntheticLocalSource(varname)
729:         result = VariableTracker.build(self.root_tx, example_value, source)
730:         # Realize the VT because we will delete the guards on it in the next line.
731:         result = result.realize()
732:         TracingContext.get().guards_context.dynamo_guards.remove_guards_with_source(
733:             source
734:         )
735:         return result

741:         for hook in reversed(self.cleanup_hooks):
742:             hook()
743:         self.cleanup_hooks.clear()

793:     @contextlib.contextmanager
794:     def subtracer(
795:         self, source_target: Optional[Target], prior_tracer: "SubgraphTracer"
796:     ) -> Generator[fx.Tracer, None, None]:
797:         new_scope_ctx = enter_new_scope()
798:         try:
799:             if prior_tracer:
800:                 # Lineage MUST stay preserved
801:                 assert prior_tracer.parent is self.current_tracer
802:             new_scope_ctx.__enter__()
803:             tracer = (
804:                 prior_tracer
805:                 if prior_tracer
806:                 else SubgraphTracer(
807:                     self,
808:                     parent=self.current_tracer,
809:                     source_target=source_target,
810:                     is_export=self.current_tracer.is_export,
811:                 )
812:             )
813:             self.tracers.append(tracer)
814:             yield tracer
815:         finally:
816:             new_scope_ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)
817:             self.tracers.pop()

901:         assert keys

910:     def new_var(self, name: str = "tmp") -> str:
911:         existing = set(self.code_options["co_varnames"])
912:         # In common case, this will be O(1)
913:         while True:
914:             var = f"{name}_{next(self.unique_var_id)}"
915:             if var not in existing:
916:                 self.code_options["co_varnames"] += (var,)
917:                 return var
918: 
919:     def update_co_names(self, name: str) -> None:
920:         """Ensure self.code_options.co_names contains name"""
921:         if name not in self.code_options["co_names"]:
922:             self.code_options["co_names"] += (name,)
923: 
924:     @staticmethod
925:     def module_key_name(*names: Any) -> str:
926:         # create a new unique name
927:         name = "_".join(map(str, names))
928:         # Strip the guard lookup L/G access
929:         name = re.sub(r"^[GL]\['?(.*?)'?\]$", r"\1", name)
930:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz[123].qkv with abc.xyz_123.qkv
931:         name = re.sub(r"\[(\d+)\]", r"_\g<1>", name)
932:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz_123.qkv with abc_xyz_123_qkv
933:         name = re.sub(r"[^a-zA-Z0-9]", "_", name)
934: 
935:         if not name or not name[0].isalpha():
936:             name = "sub" + name
937: 
938:         return name

940:     def register_static_attr_and_return_proxy(
941:         self, attr_prefix: str, attr_value: Any
942:     ) -> fx.Proxy:
943:         attr_name = get_unique_name_wrt(attr_prefix, self.nn_modules)
944:         # TODO `nn_modules` has been historically overloaded to store a lot more
945:         # than just nn module objects, fix that.
946:         self.nn_modules[attr_name] = attr_value
947:         proxy = self.create_proxy("get_attr", attr_name, (), {})
948:         set_example_value(proxy.node, attr_value)
949:         return proxy
950: 
951:     def register_attr_or_module(
952:         self,
953:         target: Union[torch.nn.Module, torch.Tensor, Any],
954:         *names: Any,
955:         **options: Any,
956:     ) -> VariableTracker:
957:         if is_dynamic_nn_module(target, self.export):
958:             # Instead of returning UnspecializedNNModuleVariable, call
959:             # VariableTracker.build so that it is tracked for mutation.
960:             return VariableTracker.build(self.current_tx, target, **options)
961: 
962:         options = dict(options)
963:         assert "source" in options
964:         source = options["source"]
965:         assert not isinstance(source, ParamBufferSource)
966: 
967:         if isinstance(target, torch.Tensor):
968:             tracer = self.current_tracer
969:             if not self.is_root_tracer():
970:                 # For higher order ops, we don't want to insert the get_attr in
971:                 # innermost graph. Instead, we want to raise the params/buffers
972:                 # as inputs to the higher-order graph, and register them as
973:                 # get_attrs in the root tracer.
974: 
975:                 # Note that Dynamo will still call lift_tracked_freevar_to_input
976:                 # when these inputs are encountered for the inner graph. The
977:                 # only difference is what happens at the root tracer for
978:                 # nn.Parameters vs free inputs. The free inputs are registered
979:                 # as placeholders in the root graph, whereas the nn.Parameters
980:                 # are registered as get_attr nodes in the root graph.
981:                 tracer = self.root_tracer
982: 
983:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:
984:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None
985:                 self.param_name_to_source[module_key] = source
986: 
987:                 # Check if the attr has already been registered. This can happen
988:                 # when two different sources point to the same tensor.
989:                 assert self.root_tx is not None
990:                 if target in self.root_tx.output.side_effects:
991:                     return self.root_tx.output.side_effects[target]
992: 
993:                 if get_static_address_type(target) == "guarded" and not isinstance(
994:                     source, NumpyTensorSource
995:                 ):
996:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.ID_MATCH))
997:                 elif not is_constant_source(source):
998:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.TENSOR_MATCH))
999: 
1000:                 vt = wrap_fx_proxy(
1001:                     self.root_tx,
1002:                     tracer.create_proxy("get_attr", module_key, (), {}),
1003:                     example_value=target,
1004:                     **options,
1005:                 )
1006: 
1007:                 # Track the object so to avoid duplicate registration in case of
1008:                 # different sources pointing to the same tensor object.
1009:                 vt = self.root_tx.output.side_effects.track_object_existing(target, vt)
1010: 
1011:                 assert "tensor_dict" not in vt.as_proxy().node.meta
1012:                 vt.as_proxy().node.meta["tensor_dict"] = _extract_tensor_dict(target)
1013: 
1014:                 return vt
1015: 
1016:         elif isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):
1017:             assert isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module)
1018: 
1019:             if source:
1020:                 install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.NN_MODULE))
1021: 
1022:                 def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:
1023:                     return NNModuleVariable(type(target), module_key, target, **options)
1024: 
1025:             else:
1026:                 # This is Dynamo created graph module, e.g., graph module coming
1027:                 # from higher order ops. NNModuleVariable tracker can't be
1028:                 # sourceless, so let's return a unspecializedNNModule variable
1029:                 # tracker.
1030:                 def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:
1031:                     return variables.UnspecializedNNModuleVariable(target, **options)
1032: 
1033:         elif isinstance(target, (torch.SymInt, torch.SymFloat)):
1034:             # HACKY CODE REGION BEGIN
1035:             # WE ARE PIGGYBACKING ON EXISTING INFRA TO REGISTER ATTRS
1036:             # This ultimately gets written to self.nn_modules, which is unfortunate
1037:             # Attrs that are tenors and symints and such need to be migrated to have their
1038:             # own storage
1039:             # alas, this is like this for now
1040: 
1041:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:
1042:                 return SymNodeVariable.create(
1043:                     self,
1044:                     self.create_proxy("get_attr", module_key, (), {}),
1045:                     sym_num=target,
1046:                     **options,
1047:                 )
1048: 
1049:             # HACKY CODE REGION END
1050:         else:
1051: 
1052:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:
1053:                 self.output.update_co_names(module_key)
1054:                 self.global_scope[module_key] = target
1055:                 return VariableTracker.build(
1056:                     self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
1057:                     target,
1058:                     ConstantSource(source_name=module_key),
1059:                 )
1060: 
1061:         for k, v in self.nn_modules.items():
1062:             if v is target:
1063:                 # it already exists
1064:                 return wrap_name(k)
1065: 
1066:         name = OutputGraph.module_key_name(*names)
1067:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, self.global_scope)
1068:         self.nn_modules[name] = target
1069:         if isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):
1070: 
1071:             def register_leaf_name(leaf_name: str) -> None:
1072:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None
1073:                 new_source = ParamBufferSource(source, leaf_name)
1074:                 new_name = f"{name}.{leaf_name}"
1075:                 self.param_name_to_source[new_name] = new_source
1076:                 if isinstance(source, LocalSource):
1077:                     self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn[
1078:                         OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name())
1079:                     ] = leaf_name
1080: 
1081:             # annoying, but there are cases when we do not have parameters
1082:             # see test_nn_moduledict_contains
1083:             if hasattr(target, "_parameters"):
1084:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_parameters():
1085:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)
1086:             if hasattr(target, "_buffers"):
1087:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_buffers():
1088:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)
1089: 
1090:         return wrap_name(name)
1091: 
1092:     def handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(
1093:         self, tx: "InstructionTranslatorBase"
1094:     ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], dict[Source, Source]]:
1095:         # If list inputs are stolen, but still needed after the function call, create aliases to keep them alive
1096:         maybe_gm = self.local_scope.get("self")
1097:         stolen_list_names = get_locals_to_steal(maybe_gm)
1098:         if not stolen_list_names:
1099:             return [], {}
1100: 
1101:         alias_insts = []
1102:         needs_alias: dict[str, list[VariableTracker]] = {}
1103: 
1104:         queue = [
1105:             *tx.stack,
1106:             *tx.symbolic_locals.values(),
1107:             *self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations.keys(),
1108:         ]
1109: 
1110:         while queue:
1111:             x = queue.pop()
1112:             if isinstance(x, BaseListVariable):
1113:                 assert isinstance(x.items, list)
1114:                 queue += x.items
1115:                 continue
1116: 
1117:             if not (
1118:                 (
1119:                     x not in self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations
1120:                     or isinstance(x.mutation_type, AttributeMutationExisting)
1121:                 )
1122:                 and isinstance(x.source, GetItemSource)
1123:                 and isinstance(x.source.base, LocalSource)
1124:                 and x.source.base.local_name in stolen_list_names
1125:             ):
1126:                 continue
1127: 
1128:             stolen_name = x.source.base.local_name
1129:             if stolen_name not in needs_alias:
1130:                 needs_alias[stolen_name] = []
1131:             needs_alias[stolen_name].append(x)
1132: 
1133:         visited = {}
1134:         overridden_sources: dict[Source, Source] = {}
1135:         for arg in self.graphargs:
1136:             if not (
1137:                 isinstance(arg._example, list)
1138:                 and isinstance(arg.source, LocalSource)
1139:                 and arg.source.local_name in needs_alias
1140:             ):
1141:                 continue
1142: 
1143:             # arg is a list that will be cleared by the compiled function
1144:             list_name = arg.source.local_name
1145:             assert list_name in self.code_options["co_varnames"]
1146:             for x in needs_alias[list_name]:
1147:                 # Skip if already handled.
1148:                 if x.source in overridden_sources:
1149:                     continue
1150: 
1151:                 # A small codegen optimization because we might have different
1152:                 # VariableTrackers that share the same source.
1153:                 list_idx = x.source.index  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1154:                 if list_idx not in visited:
1155:                     alias_name = self.new_var(
1156:                         f"{list_name}_ref"
1157:                     )  # self.new_var already adds unique id suffix
1158: 
1159:                     visited[list_idx] = alias_name
1160:                     # bytecode of `alias_name = list_name[list_idx]`
1161:                     alias_insts.extend(
1162:                         [
1163:                             create_instruction("LOAD_FAST", argval=list_name),
1164:                             create_load_const(list_idx),
1165:                             create_instruction("BINARY_SUBSCR"),
1166:                             create_instruction("STORE_FAST", argval=alias_name),
1167:                         ]
1168:                     )
1169: 
1170:                 # operate on alias, handled by suffix codegen
1171:                 old_source = x.source
1172:                 overridden_sources[old_source] = LocalSource(visited[list_idx])
1173: 
1174:         # NOTE: we need `overridden_sources` because (1) we want to codegen for
1175:         # these list items to use the new local source, but (2) we want to avoid
1176:         # updating `source` in place because that might break invariants in
1177:         # other parts of Dynamo like guards.
1178:         return alias_insts, overridden_sources
1179: 
1180:     def _get_stack_values_to_restore(
1181:         self, tx: "InstructionTranslatorBase", stack_pops: int
1182:     ) -> tuple[list[VariableTracker], list[str], StackLocalsMetadata]:
1183:         """
1184:         Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.
1185: 
1186:         Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all VTs in the tx's stack.
1187: 
1188:         NullVariables in stack/locals will NOT be restored, unless they are the top `stack_pops`
1189:         elements of the stack - it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top
1190:         `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.
1191: 
1192:         Returns:
1193:             - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored
1194:             - restore_vars: names of locals corresponding to the locals part of `stack_values`
1195:             - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals
1196:                 (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)
1197:         """
1198:         tx.prune_dead_locals()
1199: 
1200:         stack_values = []
1201:         meta = StackLocalsMetadata()
1202: 
1203:         # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they
1204:         # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes
1205:         for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):
1206:             variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(value)
1207:             # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack
1208:             if len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops:
1209:                 stack_values.append(value)
1210:                 continue
1211:             if isinstance(value, NullVariable):
1212:                 meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)
1213:             else:
1214:                 stack_values.append(value)
1215:             if isinstance(value, ContextWrappingVariable):
1216:                 target_values = (
1217:                     () if value.target_values is None else tuple(value.target_values)
1218:                 )
1219:                 # NOTE: track index in stack after NULLs have been removed
1220:                 meta.stack_ctx_args.append((len(stack_values) - 1, target_values))
1221:                 meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig.append(i)
1222: 
1223:         # Add all the local vars to the "stack" so restore at the end
1224:         restore_vars: list[str] = []
1225:         val_to_names: dict[VariableTracker, list[str]] = {}
1226:         # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),
1227:         # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals
1228:         # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the
1229:         # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here
1230:         # is only needed for partial graphs.
1231:         # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,
1232:         # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume
1233:         # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.
1234:         for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():
1235:             # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching
1236:             # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_names.
1237:             # This will in turn result in spurious variables showing up in the graph.
1238:             # This was very tricky to debug. For an example, dump the graph at call_user_compiler
1239:             # while running test_subgraphs.py
1240:             if isinstance(v.source, LocalSource) and v.source.local_name == k:
1241:                 continue  # no need to restore initial state
1242:             if isinstance(v, CellVariable) and v.local_name == k:
1243:                 continue  # no need to restore initial state
1244:             # Do not load variable if it is NULL.
1245:             if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):
1246:                 # Continuation function will load the NULL for v.
1247:                 if type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v):
1248:                     meta.locals_null_keys.append(k)
1249:                     continue
1250:             else:
1251:                 # A variable should never be NULL in < 3.12
1252:                 assert not type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v)
1253:             if isinstance(v, ContextWrappingVariable):
1254:                 target_values = (
1255:                     () if v.target_values is None else tuple(v.target_values)
1256:                 )
1257:                 meta.locals_ctx_args.append((k, target_values))
1258:             if v not in val_to_names:
1259:                 val_to_names[v] = []
1260:             val_to_names[v].append(k)
1261:         for v in val_to_names.keys():
1262:             restore_vars.extend(val_to_names[v])
1263:             stack_values.extend([v] * len(val_to_names[v]))
1264: 
1265:         return stack_values, restore_vars, meta
1266: 
1267:     def compile_subgraph(
1268:         self,
1269:         tx: "InstructionTranslatorBase",
1270:         reason: GraphCompileReason,
1271:         partial_convert: bool = False,
1272:         stack_pops: int = 0,
1273:     ) -> list[StackLocalsMetadata]:
1274:         """
1275:         Compiles the current subgraph, with inputs w.r.t. self.root_tx, and codegens:
1276:             - Call the compiled subgraph
1277:             - Apply side effects
1278:             - Codegen stack and locals
1279:             - Store the locals
1280: 
1281:         Python does not allow NULL to be an arg to a function, so we do not codegen NULLs on the stack,
1282:         unless the value is one of the top `stack_pops` values on the stack (these values are expected to be
1283:         popped immediately after this generated code. The prologue of the resume function is expected to restore
1284:         any dropped NULLs.
1285: 
1286:         Returns stack indices and locals keys where we dropped NULLs, and where we found inactive context manager objects.
1287:         """
1288: 
1289:         assert self.root_tx is not None
1290: 
1291:         # FIXME temporary assert to make sure we're not accidentally compiling nested graph breaks
1292:         # before we're done the full implementation
1293:         assert self.root_tx is tx
1294: 
1295:         # bytecode tracing has finished. Pop the context manager for dynamo_timed
1296:         self.mark_bytecode_tracing_stop()
1297: 
1298:         self.partial_convert = partial_convert
1299:         self.compile_subgraph_reason = reason
1300:         self.should_exit = True
1301: 
1302:         log.debug("COMPILING GRAPH due to %s", reason)
1303: 
1304:         # prefix instructions (Python 3.11+)
1305:         prefix_insts: list[Instruction] = []
1306:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):
1307:             for inst in tx.prefix_insts:
1308:                 if inst.opname == "MAKE_CELL":
1309:                     prefix_insts.append(
1310:                         create_instruction("MAKE_CELL", argval=inst.argval)
1311:                     )
1312:                 elif inst.opname == "COPY_FREE_VARS":
1313:                     prefix_insts.append(
1314:                         create_instruction(
1315:                             "COPY_FREE_VARS", arg=len(tx.code_options["co_freevars"])
1316:                         )
1317:                     )
1318:                 else:
1319:                     prefix_insts.append(copy.copy(inst))
1320:         self.add_output_instructions(prefix_insts)
1321: 
1322:         assert not (self.pregraph_bytecode and self.export), (
1323:             "export does not support pregraph_bytecode"
1324:         )
1325:         self.add_output_instructions(self.pregraph_bytecode)
1326: 
1327:         alias_insts, overridden_sources = self.handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(
1328:             self.root_tx
1329:         )
1330:         self.add_output_instructions(alias_insts)
1331: 
1332:         # Exit from all context manager variables to make sure global state is restored
1333:         for block in reversed(self.root_tx.block_stack):
1334:             block.exit(self.root_tx, is_graph_break=reason.graph_break)
1335: 
1336:         self.cleanup_graph()
1337: 
1338:         # stack values and restore vars for each frame are pushed in reverse order
1339:         # i.e. last element corresponds to root frame, first element corresponds to current frame
1340:         all_stack_values = []
1341:         all_restore_vars = []
1342:         all_stack_locals_metas = []
1343:         cur_tx: Optional[InstructionTranslatorBase] = tx
1344:         while True:
1345:             assert cur_tx is not None
1346:             # this should have been checked by the caller
1347:             assert all(block.can_restore() for block in cur_tx.block_stack)
1348:             stack_values, restore_vars, meta = self._get_stack_values_to_restore(
1349:                 cur_tx, stack_pops
1350:             )
1351:             all_stack_values.append(stack_values)
1352:             all_restore_vars.append(restore_vars)
1353:             all_stack_locals_metas.append(meta)
1354:             if cur_tx is self.root_tx:
1355:                 break
1356:             cur_tx = tx.parent
1357: 
1358:         # Use nn.Module "proxies" in the constructed GraphModule so that
1359:         # the resulting GM does not hold additional strong references to the original modules.
1360:         # This prevents a strong ref cycle where Dynamo created code holds on to references
1361:         # to modules that also have Dynamo code cache invalidation checks.
1362:         # When cache invalidation runs, the generated GM will be invalidated, which also deletes
1363:         # the proxies.
1364:         nn_modules_proxies = {
1365:             name: nn_module_proxy(mod) for name, mod in self.nn_modules.items()
1366:         }
1367:         root = FakeRootModule(nn_modules_proxies)
1368: 
1369:         from .decorators import disable
1370: 
1371:         # to handle random calls
1372:         if len(self.random_calls) > 0:
1373:             random_calls_instructions = []
1374:             self.random_values_var = self.new_var("random_values")
1375:             rand_fn = disable(
1376:                 _get_gen_rand_values_fn(self.random_calls),
1377: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL876-1376"}

{"content":"1377:                 reason="do not trace into Dynamo rng recovery function",
1378:             )
1379:             rand_fn_name = self.install_global("__gen_rand_values", rand_fn)
1380:             codegen = PyCodegen(
1381:                 self.root_tx, root, overridden_sources=overridden_sources
1382:             )
1383:             random_calls_instructions.extend(
1384:                 codegen.load_function_name(rand_fn_name, True)
1385:             )
1386:             random_calls_instructions.extend(create_call_function(0, False))
1387:             random_calls_instructions.append(
1388:                 codegen.create_store(self.random_values_var),
1389:             )
1390:             self.add_output_instructions(random_calls_instructions)
1391: 
1392:         # call compiled fx graph
1393:         graph_output_var = None
1394:         stored_graph_output_var = False
1395:         root_stack_values = all_stack_values[-1]
1396:         if (
1397:             self.root_tx is tx
1398:             and root_stack_values
1399:             and all(
1400:                 not isinstance(
1401:                     v,
1402:                     (
1403:                         UnspecializedPythonVariable,
1404:                         NumpyNdarrayVariable,
1405:                         TensorWithTFOverrideVariable,
1406:                     ),
1407:                 )
1408:                 and not (isinstance(v, SymNodeVariable) and v.python_type() is float)
1409:                 for v in root_stack_values
1410:             )
1411:             and all(isinstance(x, TensorVariable) for x in root_stack_values)
1412:             and len(set(root_stack_values)) == len(root_stack_values)
1413:             and self.side_effects.is_empty()
1414:             and not tx.debug_locals
1415:             and not self.backward_state
1416:             and not all_stack_locals_metas[-1].stack_null_idxes
1417:             and not all_stack_locals_metas[-1].locals_null_keys
1418:         ):
1419:             # optimization to generate better code in a common case
1420:             self.add_output_instructions(
1421:                 self.compile_and_call_fx_graph(
1422:                     tx, list(reversed(root_stack_values)), root
1423:                 )
1424:                 + [create_instruction("UNPACK_SEQUENCE", arg=len(root_stack_values))]
1425:             )
1426:         else:
1427:             graph_output_var = self.new_var("graph_out")
1428:             # load stack values in a flat manner for now - will likely change later.
1429:             stack_values_flat = [
1430:                 val for vals in reversed(all_stack_values) for val in vals
1431:             ]
1432:             pass1 = PyCodegen(
1433:                 self.root_tx,
1434:                 root,
1435:                 graph_output_var,
1436:                 overridden_sources=overridden_sources,
1437:             )
1438:             self.codegen_suffix(tx, stack_values_flat, pass1)
1439: 
1440:             # Use `pass1.uses` to selectively cache multi-user variables into a
1441:             # temporary local source. This (a). speeds up loading VTs with long
1442:             # chained source, and (b). avoids redundantly saving single-user VT
1443:             # into a temporary local.
1444:             tempvars = {}  # type: ignore[var-annotated]
1445:             for val, count in pass1.uses.items():
1446:                 # If it's already a local source, no need to cache it
1447:                 if count > 1 and not istype(val, (SyntheticLocalSource, LocalSource)):
1448:                     tempvars[val] = None
1449:             pass2 = PyCodegen(
1450:                 self.root_tx,
1451:                 root,
1452:                 graph_output_var,
1453:                 tempvars=tempvars,
1454:                 overridden_sources=overridden_sources,
1455:             )
1456:             self.codegen_suffix(tx, stack_values_flat, pass2)
1457: 
1458:             output = []
1459:             if count_calls(self.graph) != 0 or len(pass2.graph_outputs) != 0:
1460:                 output.extend(
1461:                     self.compile_and_call_fx_graph(tx, pass2.graph_output_vars(), root)
1462:                 )
1463: 
1464:                 if len(pass2.graph_outputs) != 0:
1465:                     output.append(pass2.create_store(graph_output_var))
1466:                     stored_graph_output_var = True
1467:                 else:
1468:                     output.append(create_instruction("POP_TOP"))
1469:             else:
1470:                 # NB: Important to run compiler collective even when there is
1471:                 # a graph break
1472:                 self.run_compiler_collective()
1473:             self.add_output_instructions(output + pass2.get_instructions())
1474: 
1475:         # restore all the live local vars of the root
1476:         local_restore_cg = PyCodegen(
1477:             self.root_tx, overridden_sources=overridden_sources
1478:         )
1479:         # TODO this local restoration should be removed when fully implementing nested graph breaks
1480:         self.add_output_instructions(
1481:             [
1482:                 local_restore_cg.create_store(var)
1483:                 for var in reversed(all_restore_vars[-1])
1484:             ]
1485:         )
1486: 
1487:         if graph_output_var and stored_graph_output_var:
1488:             self.add_output_instructions(
1489:                 [local_restore_cg.create_delete(graph_output_var)]
1490:             )
1491: 
1492:         if self.export:
1493:             from torch.export._trace import _ExportModuleSpecTrackerDict
1494: 
1495:             potential_side_effects = []
1496:             for var in self.side_effects._get_modified_vars():
1497:                 if hasattr(var, "mutation_type"):
1498:                     mut_type = var.mutation_type
1499:                     # Make sure to skip codegen specific mutations
1500:                     if isinstance(
1501:                         mut_type, (AttributeMutationExisting, ValueMutationExisting)
1502:                     ):
1503:                         # export uses tracepoint pass to dump submodule inp/out spec
1504:                         # into global state, so we filter it here
1505:                         if not (
1506:                             isinstance(var, UserDefinedDictVariable)
1507:                             and isinstance(var.value, _ExportModuleSpecTrackerDict)
1508:                         ):
1509:                             potential_side_effects.append(var)
1510: 
1511:             side_effect_refs = [
1512:                 _get_source_debug_name(var.source) for var in potential_side_effects
1513:             ]
1514: 
1515:             if len(side_effect_refs):
1516:                 warnings.warn(
1517:                     f"While exporting, we found certain side effects happened in the model.forward. "
1518:                     f"Here are the list of potential sources you can double check: {side_effect_refs}"
1519:                 )
1520: 
1521:         return all_stack_locals_metas
1522: 
1523:     def codegen_suffix(
1524:         self,
1525:         tx: "InstructionTranslatorBase",
1526:         stack_values: list[VariableTracker],
1527:         cg: PyCodegen,
1528:     ) -> None:
1529:         # NOTE: `codegen_save_tempvars` must run first to update `source` fields
1530:         # for variables with `AttributeMutationNew`, as they don't implement
1531:         # `reconstruct` themselves.
1532:         self.side_effects.codegen_save_tempvars(cg)
1533:         if self.backward_state:
1534:             assert not self.export
1535:             for name, val in self.backward_state.items():
1536:                 cg(val)
1537:                 assert self.backward_state_var is not None
1538:                 cg.append_output(cg.create_load(self.backward_state_var))
1539:                 cg.store_attr(name)
1540:         self.side_effects.codegen_hooks(cg)
1541: 
1542:         # Return variables used for logging at the end
1543:         for debug_var, args in tx.debug_locals:
1544:             cg.add_push_null(lambda: cg(debug_var))
1545:             for arg in args:
1546:                 cg(arg)
1547:             cg.extend_output(create_call_function(len(args), False))
1548:             cg.extend_output([create_instruction("POP_TOP")])
1549: 
1550:         cg.restore_stack(stack_values, value_from_source=not tx.export)
1551:         self.side_effects.codegen_update_mutated(cg)
1552: 
1553:     def cleanup_graph(self) -> None:
1554:         """
1555:         Remove "creation_timestamp" from node meta
1556: 
1557:         Remove this pattern from the graph:
1558:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(False)
1559:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(True)
1560:         """
1561:         assert self.should_exit
1562:         nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)
1563:         for node in nodes:
1564:             node.meta.pop("creation_timestamp", None)
1565: 
1566:         grad_enabled = torch.is_grad_enabled()
1567:         for node1, node2 in zip(nodes, nodes[1:]):
1568:             if (
1569:                 node1.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled
1570:                 and tuple(node1.args) == (not grad_enabled,)
1571:                 and not node1._erased
1572:             ):
1573:                 grad_enabled = node1.args[0]
1574:                 if (
1575:                     node2.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled
1576:                     and tuple(node2.args) == (not grad_enabled,)
1577:                     and not node2._erased
1578:                 ):
1579:                     grad_enabled = node2.args[0]
1580:                     self.graph.erase_node(node1)
1581:                     self.graph.erase_node(node2)
1582: 
1583:     def get_graph_sizes_structured(self) -> dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]]:
1584:         ret: dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]] = {}
1585:         for node in self.graph.nodes:
1586:             example_value = node.meta.get("example_value", None)
1587:             if isinstance(example_value, torch._subclasses.FakeTensor):
1588:                 size = example_value.size()
1589:                 ret[node.name] = [s if isinstance(s, int) else repr(s) for s in size]
1590:         return ret
1591: 
1592:     def get_graph_sizes(self, name: str) -> str:
1593:         graph_sizes_str = "TRACED GRAPH TENSOR SIZES\n"
1594:         graph_sizes_str += f"===== {name} =====\n"
1595:         for node in self.graph.nodes:
1596:             example_value = node.meta.get("example_value", None)
1597:             if isinstance(example_value, torch._subclasses.FakeTensor):
1598:                 size = example_value.size()
1599:                 graph_sizes_str += f"{node.name}: {tuple(size)}\n"
1600:                 concrete_size = []
1601:                 has_symint = False
1602:                 for sz in size:
1603:                     if isinstance(sz, int):
1604:                         concrete_size.append(sz)
1605:                     elif isinstance(sz, torch.SymInt):
1606:                         has_symint = True
1607:                         concrete_size.append(sz.node.hint)
1608:                     else:
1609:                         break
1610:                 else:
1611:                     if has_symint:
1612:                         graph_sizes_str += (
1613:                             f"{node.name} (concrete): {tuple(concrete_size)}\n"
1614:                         )
1615:         return graph_sizes_str
1616: 
1617:     @contextlib.contextmanager
1618:     def restore_global_state(self) -> Any:
1619:         """
1620:         Momentarily restores the global state to what it was prior to tracing the current output
1621:         """
1622:         prior_global_state = self.tracing_context.global_context.copy_graphstate()
1623:         current_global_state: dict[str, tuple[Any, bool]] = {}
1624:         self.save_global_state(out=current_global_state)
1625:         try:
1626:             # Set to state prior to tracing the graph
1627:             self.tracing_context.global_context.restore_graphstate(prior_global_state)
1628:             yield
1629:         finally:
1630:             # Reset to state at the current time (e.g. before calling the user compiler)
1631:             self.tracing_context.global_context.restore_graphstate(
1632:                 GlobalContextCheckpointState(current_global_state)
1633:             )
1634: 
1635:     def run_compiler_collective(self) -> None:
1636:         tx = self.root_tx
1637:         assert tx is not None
1638:         if (ds := tx.distributed_state) is not None and ds.all_states is None:
1639:             compile_pg = ds.compile_pg
1640:             log.info("compiler_collective %s", ds.local_state)
1641:             torch._logging.trace_structured(
1642:                 "artifact",
1643:                 metadata_fn=lambda: {
1644:                     "name": "compiler_collective",
1645:                     "encoding": "string",
1646:                 },
1647:                 payload_fn=lambda: ds.local_state.render(),
1648:             )
1649:             device_types = compile_pg._device_types
1650:             assert len(device_types) == 1, (
1651:                 "Expect only one device type but got {}".format("+".join(device_types))
1652:             )
1653:             with (
1654:                 get_interface_for_device(device_types.pop()).device(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1655:                     compile_pg.rank() % torch.accelerator.device_count()
1656:                 ),
1657:                 dynamo_timed("compiler_collective", log_pt2_compile_event=True),
1658:             ):
1659:                 all_states: list[Any] = [None] * compile_pg.size()
1660:                 dist.all_gather_object(all_states, ds.local_state, group=compile_pg)
1661:                 ds.all_states = all_states
1662:             # Clear speculation log, because are tracing may diverge due to
1663:             # this information from the compiler collective
1664:             tx.speculation_log.clear()
1665:             raise exc.CompileCollectiveRestartAnalysis
1666: 
1667:     def compile_and_call_fx_graph(
1668:         self,
1669:         tx: "InstructionTranslatorBase",
1670:         rv: list[VariableTracker],
1671:         root: FakeRootModule,
1672:     ) -> list[Instruction]:
1673:         """
1674:         Generate code from self.graph and return the Instruction()s to
1675:         call that generated code.
1676: 
1677:         Code is generated w.r.t. self.root_tx.
1678:         tx is only used for preserving GraphModule metadata
1679:         """
1680:         with torch._guards.TracingContext.clear_frame():
1681:             from .decorators import disable
1682: 
1683:             assert self.should_exit
1684: 
1685:             self.run_compiler_collective()
1686: 
1687:             name = unique_id("__compiled_fn", with_uuid=True)
1688: 
1689:             assert isinstance(rv, list)
1690:             assert isinstance(root, FakeRootModule)
1691: 
1692:             output_node = self.create_node(
1693:                 "output",
1694:                 "output",
1695:                 (self.current_tracer.create_arg(tuple(x.as_proxy() for x in rv)),),
1696:                 {},
1697:             )
1698:             sub_gms = self.dedup_pass()
1699:             root.add_nn_modules(sub_gms)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
1700: 
1701:             self.current_tracer._maybe_preserve_original_meta(tx, output_node)
1702:             if not config.do_not_emit_runtime_asserts:
1703:                 # There is a rare scenario where codegen_suffix adds a new entry
1704:                 # to self.nn_modules while `root` knows only about the
1705:                 # nn_modules at the time of its creation. This causes failures
1706:                 # while creating the graph module because self.graph and root
1707:                 # are out of sync. This only happens for `get_attr` nodes, so
1708:                 # here we clean up the get_attr nodes that are unused.
1709:                 self.remove_unused_get_attr_nodes()
1710:                 insert_deferred_runtime_asserts(
1711:                     fx.GraphModule(root, self.graph),
1712:                     self.shape_env,
1713:                     name,
1714:                     export=self.export,
1715:                 )
1716:             # NB: deferred runtime asserts can keep graphargs live, so make sure
1717:             # those are inserted before pruning
1718:             self.remove_unused_graphargs()
1719:             ncalls = count_calls(self.graph)
1720:             counters["stats"]["calls_captured"] += ncalls
1721: 
1722:             self.remove_tensorify_specialized_graphargs()
1723: 
1724:             # free a bit of memory
1725:             self.real_value_cache.clear()
1726: 
1727:             gm = _make_graph_module(root, self.graph)
1728: 
1729:             # Saved tensors hooks are not used by the graph.
1730:             # GraphModule by default only copies used in the graph submodules.
1731:             # Copying them into the result graph manually.
1732:             if self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names:
1733:                 for subgraph_name in self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names:
1734:                     setattr(gm, subgraph_name, getattr(root, subgraph_name))
1735: 
1736:             for register_finalizer in self.register_finalizer_fns:
1737:                 register_finalizer(gm)
1738: 
1739:             gm._backend_id = name
1740:             gm.compile_subgraph_reason = self.compile_subgraph_reason
1741:             gm.meta["dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn"] = (
1742:                 self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.copy()
1743:             )
1744:             gm.meta["dynamo_compile_id"] = self.dynamo_compile_id
1745: 
1746:             graph_code_log.debug(
1747:                 "%s",

1897:     def _call_user_compiler(
1898:         self, gm: fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[Tensor]
1899:     ) -> CompiledFn:
1900:         assert self.compiler_fn is not None
1901:         tot = 0
1902:         placeholders = []
1903:         for node in gm.graph.nodes:
1904:             if node.op in ("call_function", "call_method", "call_module"):
1905:                 tot += 1
1906:             if node.op == "placeholder":
1907:                 placeholders.append(node)
1908:         increment_op_count(tot)
1909:         for pl in placeholders:
1910:             if not hasattr(pl, "_dynamo_source"):
1911:                 arg = pl.meta["grapharg"]
1912:                 # TODO: Why isn't this stored in meta :think:
1913:                 # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640
1914:                 pl._dynamo_source = arg.source
1915: 
1916:         # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640
1917:         gm._param_name_to_source = self.param_name_to_source  # type: ignore[assignment]
1918:         gm._source_to_user_stacks = self.source_to_user_stacks  # type: ignore[assignment]
1919: 
1920:         name = (
1921:             self.compiler_fn.__name__
1922:             if hasattr(self.compiler_fn, "__name__")
1923:             else "<unknown compiler_fn>"
1924:         )
1925:         try:
1926:             _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f"calling compiler function {name}")
1927:             compiler_fn = self.compiler_fn
1928:             if config.verify_correctness:
1929:                 compiler_fn = WrapperBackend(compiler_fn)
1930:             compiled_fn = compiler_fn(gm, example_inputs)
1931:             _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f"done compiler function {name}")
1932:             assert callable(compiled_fn), "compiler_fn did not return callable"
1933:         except (TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis, ShortenTraceback):
1934:             raise
1935:         except exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback as e:
1936:             if self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph:
1937:                 raise BackendCompilerFailed(
1938:                     self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()
1939:                 ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None
1940:             unimplemented_v2_with_warning(
1941:                 e,
1942:                 self.root_tx.f_code,
1943:                 gb_type="Backend compiler exception",
1944:                 context=f"Backend: {name}\nException:{str(e)}\nTraceback:\n{self.root_tx.format_frame_summary()}",
1945:                 explanation=f"Backend compiler `{name}` failed with {str(e)}. Adding a graph break.",
1946:                 hints=[
1947:                     "Report an issue to the backend compiler repo.",
1948:                 ],
1949:             )
1950:         except SkipFrame as e:
1951:             # The backend compiler has requested that we skip the frame, instead of
1952:             # aborting execution.
1953:             raise e
1954:         except Exception as e:
1955:             raise BackendCompilerFailed(
1956:                 self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()
1957:             ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None
1958: 
1959:         signpost_event(
1960:             "dynamo",
1961:             "OutputGraph.call_user_compiler",
1962:             {
1963:                 **self.co_fields,
1964:                 "op_count": tot,
1965:                 "node_count": len(gm.graph.nodes),
1966:                 "input_count": len(placeholders),
1967:             },
1968:         )
1969: 
1970:         return compiled_fn

1996:     def remove_unused_graphargs(self) -> None:
1997:         # NB: It's OK to drop GraphArg for symbols that ended up being
1998:         # specialized iff they are not used in runtime assertions.  You don't
1999:         # even have to make a guard for it, because ShapeEnv produce_guards
2000:         # operates on tracked_fakes, which never gets pruned.
2001:         # That being said, you'll get marginally better generated
2002:         # guard code if you promote the guard into a Dynamo guard (since that
2003:         # allows for the guard to be done using C++ guards.)  If we get
2004:         # ShapeEnv guards to go into C++ guards, this will stop being a thing
2005:         # though!
2006: 
2007:         assert self.should_exit
2008: 
2009:         # Miniature DCE pass, but only for obviously trivial operations
2010:         def is_static_true(b_node: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:
2011:             if b_node is True:
2012:                 return True
2013:             if not isinstance(b_node, fx.Node):
2014:                 return False
2015:             b = b_node.meta.get("example_value")
2016:             if b is None:
2017:                 return False
2018:             if b is True:
2019:                 return True
2020:             if (
2021:                 isinstance(b, torch.SymBool)
2022:                 and (r := b.node.maybe_as_bool()) is not None
2023:             ):
2024:                 return r
2025:             # TODO: We can also technically remove all cases when the input
2026:             # doesn't have unbacked inputs, since it's all in the ShapeEnv
2027:             return False
2028: 
2029:         def is_symnode_arg(a: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:
2030:             from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes
2031: 
2032:             if isinstance(a, (int, float, bool)):
2033:                 return True
2034:             if isinstance(a, fx.Node):
2035:                 return isinstance(a.meta.get("example_value"), SymTypes)
2036:             return False
2037: 
2038:         # NB: We assume that you cannot do mutations on int/float/bool,
2039:         # because they are immutable types, and therefore is always safe to
2040:         # DCE.
2041:         def is_symnode_compute_node(node: fx.Node) -> bool:
2042:             from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes
2043: 
2044:             if node.op != "call_function":
2045:                 return False
2046:             # TODO: I don't think it's possible to have a bare int/float here?
2047:             if not isinstance(node.meta.get("example_value"), SymTypes):
2048:                 return False
2049:             # TODO: This will bail here if you ever end up with a more complicated
2050:             # computation function, like sum(list_of_ints), even though it
2051:             # should be DCE'able
2052:             if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.args):
2053:                 return False
2054:             if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.kwargs.values()):
2055:                 return False
2056:             return True
2057: 
2058:         from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_accessor_node
2059: 
2060:         for node in reversed(list(self.graph.nodes)):
2061:             if len(list(node.users)) == 0:
2062:                 if (
2063:                     node.op == "get_attr"
2064:                     or (node.op == "call_function" and node.target is operator.getitem)
2065:                     or (
2066:                         node.op == "call_function"
2067:                         and node.target is torch._check
2068:                         and is_static_true(node.args[0])
2069:                     )
2070:                     or is_symnode_compute_node(node)
2071:                     or is_accessor_node(node)
2072:                 ):
2073:                     self.remove_node(node)
2074: 
2075:         def placeholder_binds_symbol(node: fx.Node) -> Optional[sympy.Symbol]:
2076:             arg = node.meta["grapharg"]
2077:             example = arg.example
2078:             if isinstance(example, torch.SymInt) and isinstance(
2079:                 example.node.expr, sympy.Symbol
2080:             ):
2081:                 return example.node.expr
2082:             return None

2084:         def remove_unused(node: fx.Node) -> None:
2085:             log.debug("REMOVE UNUSED GRAPHARG %s", node.meta["grapharg"].source.name())
2086:             # I'm not really sure why you need to delete these from the
2087:             # node since the node is going to get removed
2088:             del node.meta["grapharg"]

2094:         def update_used_symbols(
2095:             used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol], fake: Union[torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor]
2096:         ) -> None:
2097:             used_symbols |= free_symbols(fake)
2098: 
2099:         recheck_placeholders = []
2100:         for node in self.placeholders:
2101:             binds_symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node) is not None
2102:             # Don't delete symbol bindings yet
2103:             if binds_symbol:

2106:             else:
2107:                 if not node.users and not isinstance(
2108:                     node.meta["grapharg"], BackwardStateGraphArg
2109:                 ):
2110:                     remove_unused(node)
2111:                 else:
2112:                     # Register the free symbols as uses
2113:                     arg = node.meta["grapharg"]
2114:                     if isinstance(arg, BackwardStateGraphArg):
2115:                         continue
2116:                     if isinstance(node.meta["grapharg"].example, torch.ScriptObject):
2117:                         real_script_obj = node.meta["grapharg"].example
2118:                         fake_script_obj = node.meta["grapharg"].example_strong_ref
2119:                         if not torch._library.fake_class_registry.tracing_with_real(
2120:                             real_script_obj
2121:                         ):
2122:                             flat_dict = dict(real_script_obj.__obj_flatten__())  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
2123:                             for attr in flat_dict.keys():
2124:                                 fake_attr_val = getattr(
2125:                                     fake_script_obj.wrapped_obj, attr
2126:                                 )
2127:                                 pytree.tree_map_only(
2128:                                     (torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor),
2129:                                     lambda t: update_used_symbols(used_symbols, t),
2130:                                     fake_attr_val,
2131:                                 )
2132:                         continue
2133:                     fake = (
2134:                         arg.fake_tensor if arg.fake_tensor is not None else arg.example
2135:                     )
2136:                     update_used_symbols(used_symbols, fake)
2137: 
2138:         # After removing unused graphargs, prune unused binds_symbol
2139:         for node in recheck_placeholders:
2140:             symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node)
2141:             if symbol is not None:
2142:                 if symbol not in used_symbols:
2143:                     remove_unused(node)
2144:                 else:
2145:                     # Make sure we delete later occurrences of the same symbol
2146:                     used_symbols.remove(symbol)

2148:     def remove_tensorify_specialized_graphargs(self) -> None:
2149:         # This is a pretty interesting function. Basically we have this problem
2150:         # where our compiler tends to choke when we have unused inputs. The way
2151:         # we support dynamic float arguments is by doing a joint fx pass and
2152:         # tensorifying away as many symfloats as we can. For the remaining symfloats
2153:         # we have no choice but to specialize... HOWEVER at that point in time
2154:         # we can no longer remove graph inputs. So our sledgehammer solution is to
2155:         # save the state of what inputs we should have specialized in dynamo and
2156:         # restart analysis. This function incorporates this "view from the future"
2157:         # state and specializes inputs that we know we won't be able to tensorify
2158:         # away in the joint pass. In principle we shouldn't choke on unused inputs
2159:         # and so this shouldn't be necessary. In practice CUDA graphs choke on
2160:         # unused inputs so we need this for now.
2161: 
2162:         # Import here to prevent circular import
2163:         from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import TensorifyState
2164: 
2165:         for node in self.graph.nodes:
2166:             example_value = node.meta.get("example_value")
2167:             if (
2168:                 isinstance(example_value, FakeTensor)
2169:                 and example_value.item_memo is not None
2170:                 and hasattr(example_value.item_memo.node._expr, "name")
2171:                 and all(u.target == "item" for u in node.users)
2172:                 and TensorifyState.should_specialize(
2173:                     # We use _expr instead of expr b/c we want the symbol not the replacement
2174:                     example_value.item_memo.node._expr.name
2175:                 )
2176:             ):
2177:                 for u in list(node.users):
2178:                     u.replace_all_uses_with(guard_scalar(example_value.item_memo))
2179:                     self.remove_node(u)
2180:                 self.remove_node(node)

2190:     def install_global_unsafe(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:
2191:         """
2192:         WARNING: prefer the safer `install_global_by_id/install_global`.
2193:         torch.compile instances should be independent of each other;
2194:         one footgun is to have one instance depend on the existence of
2195:         a global installed by another instance. This can happen if we mangle
2196:         a global the same way across both instances.
2197:         """
2198:         assert name not in self.installed_globals
2199:         self.installed_globals.add(name)
2200:         self.cleanups.append(CleanupHook.create(self.global_scope, name, value))
2201: 
2202:     def install_global_by_id(self, prefix: str, value: Any) -> str:
2203:         """
2204:         Installs a global if it hasn't been installed already.
2205:         This is determined by (prefix, id(value)) pair.
2206: 
2207:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.
2208:         """
2209:         # NB: need self.compile_id to distinguish this global
2210:         # from another global created in a different torch.compile instance
2211:         name = f"{prefix}_{id(value)}_c{self.compile_id}"
2212:         if name in self.installed_globals:
2213:             return name
2214:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)
2215:         return name
2216: 
2217:     def install_global(self, prefix: str, value: Any) -> str:
2218:         """
2219:         Installs a global, generating a unique name for it.
2220: 
2221:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.
2222:         """
2223:         # NB: unique_id is unique, even across torch.compile instances
2224:         name = unique_id(prefix)
2225:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)
2226:         return name

2371:     Holds an FX graph that is being traced. OutputGraph owns a SubgraphTracer
2372:     and the separation of responsibilities is that SubgraphTracer is
2373:     responsible for building the graph while OutputGraph is responsible for
2374:     compiling and executing the graph.

2489:     def create_proxy(
2490:         self,
2491:         kind: str,
2492:         target: Any,
2493:         args: Any,
2494:         kwargs: Any,
2495:         name: Optional[str] = None,
2496:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,
2497:         proxy_factory_fn: Optional[Callable[[fx.Node], fx.Proxy]] = None,
2498:     ) -> fx.Proxy:
2499:         # NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling]
2500:         # --------------------------------------------------------
2501:         # Read NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] first.
2502:         #
2503:         # Let's say we're in the middle of introspecting the body of a possibly
2504:         # nested HigherOrderOperator, and we see a free variable.
2505:         #
2506:         # There are two cases:
2507:         # 1. We see a free variable that is already tracked by Dynamo.
2508:         # 2. We see a free variable that has not been tracked by Dynamo
2509:         #
2510:         # In case 1, we call `maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input` (below)
2511:         # which will lift the freevar to be an input of this subgraph
2512:         # and also recursively lift it to be an input on the parent(s).
2513:         #
2514:         # In case 2, before the call to `create_proxy`, the InstructionTranslator
2515:         # will see the freevar when it gets loaded by Python bytecode.
2516:         # E.g. for Python 3.11 the bytecodes that may do this are LOAD_DEREF or
2517:         # LOAD_GLOBAL.
2518:         # There, the InstructionTranslator asks Dynamo to begin tracking the
2519:         # freevar by building a new Variable.
2520:         # Building a new Variable automatically lifts the freevar to be an
2521:         # input of the root SubgraphTracer.
2522:         #
2523:         # The implications for the code below are:
2524:         # - We will always be in Case 1 when we get to this code.

2529:         #   lift this free variable into inputs (recursively) of each nested
2530:         #   higher-order-op subgraph until we hit the subgraph where the free

2631:

2633:                 if kind in {"call_function", "call_method"}:
2634:                     rv.node.meta["source_fn_stack"] = self.source_fn_stack + [

2636:                     ]
2637:                 elif kind == "call_module":
2638:                     if self.parent is not None:
2639:                         # TODO can remove once inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is always True
2640:                         unimplemented_v2(
2641:                             gb_type="Invoking an nn.Module inside a HigherOrderOperator",
2642:                             context="",
2643:                             explanation="This is not supported.",
2644:                             hints=[],
2645:                         )

2647:                     rv.node.meta["source_fn_stack"] = self.source_fn_stack + [
2648:                         (
2649:                             rv.node.name,
2650:                             rv.node.meta["nn_module_stack"][target][1],

2678:     def create_node(
2679:         self,
2680:         op: str,
2681:         target: Target,
2682:         args: Any = None,
2683:         kwargs: Any = None,
2684:         name: Optional[str] = None,
2685:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,
2686:     ) -> fx.Node:

2690:             for arg in flat_args:
2691:                 if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Node):
2692:                     continue
2693:                 assert arg.graph == self.graph, (
2694:                     "create_node using arg not from this SubgraphTracer"
2695:                 )
2696: 
2697:         node = super().create_node(op, target, args, kwargs, name, type_expr)
2698:         node.meta["creation_timestamp"] = self.output_graph.timestamp
2699:         self._used_names.add(node.name)
2700:         return node

2702:     # Note: we did not override erase_node since
2703:     # we call self.graph.erase_node elsewhere
2704:     def remove_node(self, node: fx.Node) -> None:
2705:         if len(node.users) > 0:
2706:             user_graph_nodes: list[torch.fx.Node] = []
2707:             for user in node.users.keys():
2708:                 # For the case where user.graph == self.graph, that is a real bug and will raise
2709:                 # properly.
2710:                 if user.graph != self.graph:
2711:                     # This is a nested graph, which needs to be deleted.
2712:                     # If we do not do this, we will raise on attempting to remove this.
2713:                     # As we only get here during restoration cleanup, this is sound.
2714:                     user_graph_nodes.extend(reversed(list(user.graph.nodes)))
2715:             for other_graph_node in user_graph_nodes:
2716:                 other_graph_node.graph.erase_node(other_graph_node)

2718:         self.input_name_to_proxy.pop(node.name, None)

2720:     # when before=True, we will insert this input before the most recent
2721:     # inserted proxy.  This is a hack to get around an ordering problem,
2722:     # where we first insert a tensor argument, and then insert bindings
2723:     # for SymInts that may occur in the tensor argument.
2724:     # Remove this if https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/99007 gets
2725:     # fixed.
2726:     def create_graph_input(
2727:         self,
2728:         name: str,
2729:         type_expr: Any,
2730:         example_value: Any,
2731:         before: bool = False,
2732:         source: Optional[Source] = None,
2733:     ) -> fx.Proxy:
2734:         if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):
2735:             self._input_versions_at_beginning.append(example_value._version)
2736:         log.debug(
2737:             "create_graph_input %s %s %s at debug_level %s before=%s",
2738:             name,
2739:             source.name() if source is not None else "(none)",
2740:             example_value,
2741:             self.debug_level,
2742:             before,
2743:         )
2744:         if source is None:
2745:             assert self.parent is not None, (
2746:                 f"you are required to provide a source for inputs {name} example_val {example_value} on the root tracer"
2747:             )
2748: 
2749:         # Note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]
2750:         # In eager, we are generally OK with adding graph inputs whenever we
2751:         # want, because we take care of writing the bytecode that knows how
2752:         # to source all the inputs.
2753:         #
2754:         # In export, this is bad, because you want a self-contained export
2755:         # object which only depends on the inputs you explicitly passed to it.
2756:         # So we are a bit more strict about what sources can become inputs
2757:         # in export
2758:         if self.is_export and self.parent is None:
2759:             assert source is not None
2760:             if not is_from_local_source(source, only_allow_input=True):
2761:                 self.output_graph.source_to_user_stacks.setdefault(source, []).append(
2762:                     TracingContext.extract_stack()
2763:                 )

2765:         # _used_names contains the names of all the nodes in the graph,
2766:         # including intermediates. This ensures that we do not have a name
2767:         # collision.
2768:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self._used_names)
2769:         if self.input_name_to_proxy:
2770:             prev_name = next(reversed(self.input_name_to_proxy))
2771:             node = self.input_name_to_proxy[prev_name].node
2772:             if before:
2773:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(node)
2774:             else:
2775:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_after(node)

2777:             ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(None)
2778:         with ctx:
2779:             proxy = self.create_proxy("placeholder", name, (), {}, type_expr=type_expr)
2780:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)

2782:                 k, v = self.input_name_to_proxy.popitem()
2783:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy
2784:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[k] = v
2785:             else:
2786:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy

3144:     # Lookup the proxy in current tracer for each symbol in expressions of s,
3145:     # See Note [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]
3146:     def lookup_unbound_symbols(self, s: torch.SymInt) -> list[sympy.Symbol]:
3147:         free_symbols = s.node.expr.free_symbols
3148:         if len(free_symbols) == 0:
3149:             return []
3150: 
3151:         to_be_bound = []
3152:         for s0 in free_symbols:
3153:             if s0 not in self.bound_symbols:
3154:                 to_be_bound.append(s0)
3155:                 continue
3156: 
3157:             proxy = self.bound_symbols[s0]
3158:             if isinstance(proxy, LazyProxy):
3159:                 proxy = proxy()
3160:                 self.bound_symbols[s0] = proxy
3161:             assert isinstance(proxy, torch.fx.Proxy) and proxy.tracer is self, (
3162:                 f"The proxy of symbol {s0} doesn't belong to current tracer."
3163:             )
3164:         # Sort the symbols so that we can have a deterministic lifting order
3165:         return sorted(to_be_bound, key=lambda s: s.name)

3241: # NOTE: [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]
3242: # Ignoring HigherOrderOperators for a moment,
3243: # OutputGraph represents the graph being built by Dynamo that may be compiled
3244: # and executed. It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.
3245: #
3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.
3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect
3248: # the function passed to it (call this the "body function"), capture it into a
3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the
3250: # GraphModule.
3251: #
3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having
3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.
3254: #
3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:
3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer
3257: # - Executing the body function.
3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer
3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. For example:
3260: # - the OutputGraph can receive new GraphArgs (if we discover any new
3261: #   untracked Tensors)
3262: # - side effects from the body function get accumulated into
3263: #   OutputGraph.side_effects
3264: # - guards produced by the body function get accumulated into OutputGraph.guards
3265: #
3266: # The traced function has some special properties that make it easier for us
3267: # to transform later down the line:
3268: # - we lift all free variables to being inputs.
3269: #
3270: # If the introspection fails (due to the existence of graph breaks), then
3271: # we roll back the current OutputGraph state and graph break on the
3272: # HigherOrderOperator.

   174→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   175→class VariableTrackerCacheKey:
   176→    vt_id: int
   177→    # Two different source can point to the same object. However, Dynamo handles
   178→    # globals and local source differently when it comes to guards and possibly
   179→    # some other parts as well. So, cache also relies on the source.
   180→    source: Source

   195→class VariableTrackerCache:
   196→    def __init__(self) -> None:
   197→        self.cache: dict[VariableTrackerCacheKey, VariableTracker] = {}
   198→
   199→    def lookup(self, value: Any, source: Source) -> Optional[VariableTracker]:
   200→        key = VariableTrackerCacheKey(id(value), source)
   201→        if key not in self.cache:
   202→            return None
   203→        return self.cache[key]
   204→
   205→    def add(self, value: Any, source: Source, vt: VariableTracker) -> None:
   206→        key = VariableTrackerCacheKey(id(value), source)
   207→        self.cache[key] = vt
   208→
   209→    def clone(self) -> "VariableTrackerCache":
   210→        # Needed for copy and restore graph state
   211→        new_cache = VariableTrackerCache()
   212→        new_cache.cache.update(self.cache)
   213→        return new_cache
   214→
   215→    def clear(self) -> None:
   216→        self.cache.clear()

   246→class FakeRootModule(torch.nn.Module):
   247→    """Trick the constructor of fx.GraphModule"""
   248→
   249→    def __init__(self, nn_modules: dict[str, torch.nn.Module]):
   250→        super().__init__()
   251→        for k, v in nn_modules.items():
   252→            setattr(self, k, v)
   253→
   254→    def __repr__(self) -> str:
   255→        return "FakeRootModule(...)"
   256→
   257→    def add_nn_modules(self, nn_modules: dict[str, torch.nn.Module]) -> None:
   258→        for k, v in nn_modules.items():
   259→            setattr(self, k, v)

   262→class WrapperBackend:
   263→    def __init__(self, backend: CompilerFn) -> None:
   264→        self.backend: CompilerFn = backend
   265→
   266→    def __call__(
   267→        self, gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[torch.Tensor]
   268→    ) -> CompiledFn:
   269→        self.restore = checkpoint_params(gm)
   270→        self.gm = gm
   271→        copy_gm = copy.deepcopy(self.gm)
   272→        self.candidate = self.backend(copy_gm, example_inputs)
   273→
   274→        if self.candidate is None or self.candidate is self.gm.forward:
   275→            return self.gm.forward
   276→
   277→        if not config.verify_correctness:
   278→            return self.candidate
   279→
   280→        # if verify_correctness=True
   281→        try:
   282→            correct = self.gm.forward(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))
   283→            result = self.candidate(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))
   284→
   285→            # TODO: replace `same` function with the one in testing
   286→            if same(correct, result):
   287→                return self.candidate
   288→
   289→            raise RuntimeError(f"incorrect results of backend {self}")
   290→
   291→        except Exception:
   292→            log.exception("error in verify_correctness")
   293→            raise
   294→        finally:
   295→            self.restore()
   296→

   324→
   325→    export: bool = False

   327→    name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: Optional[str] = None

   332→
   333→    @property

   335→        return self._guards

   344→    """
   345→    Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions
   346→    """
   347→
   348→    stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)
   349→    locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)
   350→    stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)
   351→    stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)
   352→    locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)
   353→
   354→
   355→def get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:
   356→    # f_globals["__builtins__"] can be a dict or a module. This is an
   357→    # implementation detail -
   358→    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.
   359→
   360→    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn
   361→    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access
   362→    # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.
   363→
   364→    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points
   365→    # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.
   366→    # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the
   367→    # install_global to give us a unique dict key.
   368→
   369→    f_builtins = global_scope["__builtins__"]
   370→    if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):
   371→        f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__
   372→    return f_builtins

This records the initial torch function mode stack for guarding\n    torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode]\n    guard_on_key_order: set[Source]\n    # Map from graph input's `Source` to sizes / strides metadata\n    input_source_to_sizes_strides: dict[Source, dict[str, Any]]\n    dual_level: int\n    functorch_layers: list[torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.FuncTorchInterpreter]\n    current_device: Optional[torch.device]\n    global_state_guard: torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard\n    _guards: torch._guards.GuardsSet\n    _aotautograd_guards: list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]\n\n    export: bool = False\n    export_constraints: bool = False\n    name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: Optional[str] = None\n\n    @property\n    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n        raise AssertionError(f\"shape_env shouldn't be accessed from {type(self)}\")\n\n    @property\n    def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n        return self._guards\n\n    @property\n    def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n        return self._aotautograd_guards\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass StackLocalsMetadata:\n    \"\"\"\n    Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\n    \"\"\"\n\n    stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n\n\ndef get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    # f_globals[\"__builtins__\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\n    # implementation detail -\n    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n\n    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n    # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n\n    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n    # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n    # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n    # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n\n    f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n    if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n        f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n    return f_builtins\n\n", "numLines": 374, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 374}}
